news.Title: Two Surprising Statistics That Could Signal Explosive Growth Ahead for Amazon.
news.Content:
Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) has built a long history of earnings growth over time thanks to its e-commerce business and its cloud computing unit. On top of that, the company has been one of the early winners of this artificial intelligence (AI) boom -- it's a user and developer of AI and also offers customers access to AI products and services.

All of this has made Amazon a compelling investment for a wide variety of investors -- from those seeking a certain level of stability to those seeking growth. Amazon stock has delivered, climbing nearly 100% over the past three years.

Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. Continue В»

The good news is these good times may be far from over. In fact, two statistics, one that applies to Amazon's e-commerce business and one that applies to the cloud business, could signal explosive growth ahead for the company. Let's zoom in for a close look.

Amazon is benefiting from its investments

First, though, let's consider where Amazon stands right now. As mentioned, the company is a leader in the growth businesses of e-commerce and cloud computing . These units have helped earnings climb, and we can see through return on invested capital that Amazon has also benefited from its investments over the years.

The ROIC patterns show that Amazon generally makes wise investing decisions. This track record may boost our confidence in the company now, as it's in a key investment phase, aiming for $200 billion in capital expenditures this year. Much of this spending will support the cloud unit, Amazon Web Services (AWS), as demand for AI and non-AI services is soaring. Attention to this area is crucial because AWS actually makes up the lion's share of the company's overall profit.

Certain headwinds have weighed on Amazon and other AI stocks in recent times, however. These range from uncertainties regarding turmoil in Iran and the U.S. economic environment to questions about the longevity of the AI spending cycle. While these elements may pressure Amazon in the short term, they aren't likely to push the company off track.

And this brings me to the two statistics that signal explosive growth ahead. The first is: Worldwide, 80% of retail sales still happen in physical stores. Amazon's Andy Jassy wrote in a shareholder letter earlier this spring that he expects that to change. So, even though Amazon already is delivering annual sales of more than $700 billion, the company still has plenty of room for expansion. And since Amazon did a major overhaul of its cost structure a few years ago, it should be perfectly positioned to benefit as a greater shift from physical stores to e-commerce takes place.

Story Continues

What may happen with global IT spending

Now, here's the second statistic: 85% of global information technology spending remains on premises. That refers to investment in a company's own data centers and equipment, for example, instead of relying on a cloud service. Jassy expects this to change, and it seems very logical that this transition will unfold. Here's why: Companies can save a huge amount of time and money by turning to a cloud service instead of building out their own platforms on-site. Turning to a cloud also offers a company access to an extremely broad portfolio of products and services -- AI and non-AI. In fact, AWS is seeing high demand in both areas.

Of course, this transition to the cloud may not happen overnight, as companies that rely on their on-premises systems may continue along that path to amortize their investments. But it's very likely that many will transfer over when the time is right; and newer, younger companies may immediately opt for the cloud rather than spending on premises. This trend should gradually increase growth for AWS.

So, even though Amazon already is a market giant, it still has plenty of room to run, and that makes it an exciting tech stock to buy and hold as these two shifts supercharge growth.

news.Title: еӨ–иіҮзӢӮж’ӨеҸ°иӮЎиЎқеӨӘз©әпјҒSpaceXдёҠеёӮеҖ’ж•ё 5еӨ§ең°йӣ·жҒҗи®“зҘһи©ұзҝ»и»Ҡ
news.Content:
з”ұз§‘жҠҖеҜҢиұӘйҰ¬ж–Ҝе…Ӣеүөз«Ӣзҡ„еӨӘз©әжҺўзҙўз§‘жҠҖе…¬еҸёSpaceXпјҢй җиЁҲж–јзҫҺжқұжҷӮй–“6жңҲ12ж—ҘеңЁзҫҺеңӢйӮЈж–ҜйҒ”е…ӢдәӨжҳ“жүҖжҺӣзүҢдёҠеёӮгҖӮеёӮе ҙй җжңҹйҖҷе°ҮжҲҗзӮәиҸҜзҲҫиЎ—иҝ‘е№ҙжңҖеҸ—зҹҡзӣ®зҡ„IPOжЎҲд№ӢдёҖпјҢз”ҡиҮіеҸҜиғҪж”№еҜ«еҸІдёҠжңҖеӨ§дёҠеёӮзҙҖйҢ„пјҢеӣ жӯӨеҗёеј•е…ЁзҗғиіҮйҮ‘й—ңжіЁпјҢйғЁеҲҶеҲҶжһҗеё«иӘҚзӮәпјҢиҝ‘жңҹеӨ–иіҮжҢҒзәҢиіЈи¶…еҸ°иӮЎпјҢеҺҹеӣ д№ӢдёҖеҸҜиғҪе°ұжҳҜжҸҗеүҚзұҢжҺӘиіҮйҮ‘пјҢд»ҘеҸғиҲҮйҖҷе ҙдё–зҙҖзҙҡж–°иӮЎиӘҚиіјгҖӮ

з„¶иҖҢпјҢеңЁеёӮе ҙдёҖзүҮзңӢеӨҡиҒІжөӘдёӯпјҢд№ҹжңүе°Ҳе®¶жҸҗйҶ’жҠ•иіҮдәәжҮүеҜ©ж…Һи©•дј°йўЁйҡӘгҖӮиІЎз¶“е°Ҳж¬„дҪңе®¶AdityaRaghunathж’°ж–ҮжҢҮеҮәпјҢSpaceXйӣ–з„¶ж“ҒжңүзҒ«з®ӯзҷје°„гҖҒStarlinkиЎӣжҳҹз¶Іи·ҜеҸҠдәәе·Ҙжҷәж…§зӯүеӨҡе…ғжҘӯеӢҷпјҢдҪҶд»ҚеӯҳеңЁдә”еӨ§жҪӣеңЁйҡұжҶӮгҖӮ

йҰ–е…ҲжҳҜдј°еҖјйҒҺй«ҳзҡ„е•ҸйЎҢгҖӮеёӮе ҙеӮіиҒһSpaceXдёҠеёӮдј°еҖјдёҠзңӢ1.77е…ҶзҫҺе…ғпјҢдҪҶз ”з©¶ж©ҹж§ӢжҷЁжҳҹеғ…зөҰеҮәзҙ„7800е„„зҫҺе…ғи©•еғ№пјҢе…©иҖ…е·®и·қи¶…йҒҺдә”жҲҗпјҢзӣ®еүҚеёӮе ҙе°Қе…¶й«ҳдј°еҖјдё»иҰҒе»әз«ӢеңЁжңӘдҫҶжҲҗй•·й җжңҹпјҢиҖҢйқһзҸҫйҡҺж®өзҚІеҲ©иғҪеҠӣгҖӮж №ж“ҡжӢӣиӮЎж–Үд»¶пјҢ2025е№ҙзҮҹж”¶йҒ”187е„„зҫҺе…ғпјҢдҪҶе…Ёе№ҙиҷ§жҗҚжҺҘиҝ‘50е„„зҫҺе…ғпјҢд»Ҡе№ҙз¬¬дёҖеӯЈжӣҙиҷ§жҗҚ43е„„зҫҺе…ғпјҢе…¶дёӯAIжҘӯеӢҷжҳҜдё»иҰҒжӢ–зҙҜдҫҶжәҗгҖӮ

з¬¬дәҢй …йўЁйҡӘдҫҶиҮӘйҫҗеӨ§зҡ„иіҮйҮ‘ж¶ҲиҖ—гҖӮйӣ–з„¶жҳҹйҸҲпјҲStarlinkпјүе·Ій–Ӣе§Ӣз©©е®ҡзҚІеҲ©пјҢдҪҶдәәе·Ҙжҷәж…§жҘӯеӢҷд»ҚйңҖеӨ§йҮҸжҠ•иіҮпјҢиӢҘжңӘдҫҶз„Ўжі•й ҶеҲ©е°ҮжҠ•е…ҘиҪүеҢ–зӮәж”¶зӣҠпјҢжҢҒзәҢж“ҙеӨ§зҡ„иіҮжң¬ж”ҜеҮәжҒҗе°Үе°Қе…¬еҸёиІЎеӢҷйҖ жҲҗеЈ“еҠӣгҖӮ

з¬¬дёүжҳҜиіҮиЁҠжҸӯйңІйҖҸжҳҺеәҰдёҚи¶ігҖӮжӢӣиӮЎжӣёжҸҗеҲ°AIж–°еүөе…¬еҸё Anthropicе°Үй•·жңҹз§ҹз”ЁSpaceXзҡ„AIз®—еҠӣиіҮжәҗпјҢдҪҶйҰ¬ж–Ҝе…ӢйҡЁеҫҢиЎЁзӨәйӣҷж–№еҜҰйҡӣеҗҲдҪңеұ¬зҹӯжңҹз§ҹзҙ„пјҢдё”еҸҜжҸҗеүҚзөӮжӯўпјҢеј•зҷјеӨ–з•ҢиіӘз–‘зӣёй—ңиіҮиЁҠжҳҜеҗҰе……еҲҶжҸӯйңІгҖӮжӯӨеӨ–пјҢеёӮе ҙе°ҚStarlinkз”ЁжҲ¶жөҒеӨұзҺҮгҖҒзҒ«з®ӯжҘӯеӢҷзҚІеҲ©жЁЎејҸеҸҠAIз®—еҠӣеҲ©з”ЁзҺҮзӯүй—ңйҚөж•ёж“ҡд»Қзјәд№Ҹе®Ңж•ҙиіҮиЁҠгҖӮ

з¬¬еӣӣй …йўЁйҡӘдҫҶиҮӘжӯ·еҸІз¶“й©—гҖӮз ”з©¶йЎҜзӨәпјҢеӨ§еһӢдјҒжҘӯIPOеҫҢдёүе№ҙзҡ„е№іеқҮиЎЁзҸҫеҫҖеҫҖдёҚеҰӮеӨ§зӣӨпјҢе°Өе…¶жҳҜдј°еҖјеҒҸй«ҳзҡ„з§‘жҠҖе…¬еҸёпјҢжӣҙе®№жҳ“еҮәзҸҫе ұй…¬зҺҮиҗҪеҫҢеёӮе ҙзҡ„жғ…жіҒгҖӮ

жңҖеҫҢеүҮжҳҜе…¬еҸёжІ»зҗҶе•ҸйЎҢгҖӮйҰ¬ж–Ҝе…ӢйҖҸйҒҺи¶…зҙҡжҠ•зҘЁж¬ҠиӮЎд»ҪжҺҢжҸЎзҙ„85%жҠ•зҘЁж¬ҠпјҢж„Ҹе‘іи‘—еҚідҪҝдёҠеёӮеҫҢпјҢеӨ–йғЁиӮЎжқұе°ҚйҮҚеӨ§жұәзӯ–д»ҚйӣЈд»ҘзҷјжҸ®еҪұйҹҝеҠӣгҖӮ

е„ҳз®ЎеҰӮжӯӨпјҢйӮ„жҳҜжңүжЁӮи§ҖжҙҫдәәеЈ«зңӢеҘҪпјҢдҫӢеҰӮж–№иҲҹжҠ•иіҮ (Ark Invest) еҹ·иЎҢй•·Cathie Wood иӘҚзӮәпјҢStarlinkе·ІиӯүжҳҺе…¶е•ҶжҘӯжЁЎејҸе…·еӮҷзҚІеҲ©иғҪеҠӣпјҢиҖҢAIжҘӯеӢҷжңӘдҫҶиӢҘжҲҗеҠҹзҷјеұ•пјҢе°Үжңүж©ҹжңғеүөйҖ жӣҙеӨ§еғ№еҖјгҖӮ

news.Title: гҖҠеҸ°иӮЎзӣӨеҫҢгҖӢзӣӨдёӯVиҪүгҖҒж”¶и·Ң76й»һпјҢйҡӘе®Ҳ43K
news.Content:
MoneyDJж–°иҒһ 2026-06-11 16:08:18 ж–°иҒһдёӯеҝғ зҷјдҪҲ

еҸ°иӮЎд»Ҡ(11)ж—ҘеҸ—зҫҺиӮЎиө°ејұеҪұйҹҝеҗҢжӯҘй–ӢдҪҺпјҢдёҖеәҰйҮҚжҢ«йҖҫ1200й»һгҖҒйҡӘдәӣж‘ңз ҙ42000й»һеӨ§й—ңпјҢдёҚйҒҺйҡЁеҫҢеңЁеҸ°з©Қйӣ»еӣһз©©гҖҒжң¬жіўеј·еӢўиӮЎеңӢе·Ё*зҝ»зҙ…ж”¶жјІ2.8%пјҢжҗӯй…ҚзҫҺиӮЎйӣ»еӯҗзӣӨзҝ»зҙ…гҖҒдёҰеё¶еӢ•ж—Ҙз¶“225жҢҮж•ёиҲҮйҹ“еңӢKOSPIжҢҮж•ёиө°жҸҡпјҢеҠ дёҠйҮ‘жҺ§иӮЎеҜҢйӮҰйҮ‘гҖҒDRAMиӮЎеҚ—дәһз§‘гҖҒдәәж°ЈиӮЎзҫӨеүөгҖҒиҲӘйҒӢиӮЎй•·жҰ®з©©зӣӨдёӢпјҢжҲҗеҠҹеҢ–и§Је…ҲеүҚеӮіеҮәCPOйҮҸз”ўжҷӮзЁӢгҖҒ800 VDCйӣ»жәҗиҰҸж је•Ҷж©ҹжҒҗе»¶еҫҢзҡ„еҲ©з©әж¶ҲжҒҜеҪұйҹҝпјҢзөӮе ҙеҠ ж¬ҠжҢҮж•ёж”¶еңЁ43149.46й»һпјҢдёӢи·Ң76.08й»һжҲ–0.18%гҖҒй»‘KдёӢеҪұз·ҡйҒ”йҖҫ1100й»һпјҢжҲҗдәӨйҮҸзё®жёӣиҮі1е…Ҷ2579.76е„„е…ғгҖӮ

зҫҺеңӢзёҪзөұе·қжҷ®жҸҡиЁҖиӢҘе’Ңе№іеҚ”иӯ°жңӘиғҪйҒ”жҲҗе°ҮеҶҚж¬Ўж”»ж“ҠдјҠжң—гҖҒеё¶еӢ•жІ№еғ№и·ій«ҳпјҢеҶҚеҠ дёҠеҚҠе°Һй«”йЎһиӮЎе»¶зәҢиҝ‘дҫҶи·ҢеӢўпјҢзҫҺеңӢеӣӣеӨ§жҢҮж•ё10ж—ҘиіЈеЈ“жІүйҮҚдёҰзҡҶж”¶й»‘пјҢдёҰд»ҘиІ»еҚҠжҢҮж•ёдёӢи·Ң3.57%жңҖејұеӢўгҖӮдәһжҙІиӮЎеёӮж–№йқўпјҢеӣ дёӯжқұжғ…еӢўжғЎеҢ–пјҢжӢ–зҙҜж—Ҙз¶“225жҢҮж•ёд»ҘдҪҺзӣӨй–ӢеҮәеҫҢпјҢдёҖеәҰйҮҚж‘”йҖҫ1,800й»һпјҢд№ӢеҫҢеӣ йғЁеҲҶAIгҖҒеҚҠе°Һй«”зӣёй—ңиӮЎиө°жҸҡпјҢж”Ҝж’җж—Ҙз¶“жҢҮж•ёзҝ»зҙ…ж”¶жјІ0.06%пјӣйҹ“еңӢKOSPIжҢҮж•ёдәҰеңЁSK HYNIXиө°еј·её¶еӢ•дёӢгҖҒж”¶жјІ0.43%гҖӮ

еҸ°иӮЎд»Ҡж—Ҙиө°еӢўеҠҮзғҲйңҮзӣӘгҖҒдёҠдёӢйңҮзӣӘе№…еәҰи¶…йҒҺ1400й»һпјҢе…¶дёӯеҸ°з©Қйӣ»зӣӨдёӯзҝ»й»‘пјҢзөӮе ҙжӢүеӣһиҮіе№ізӣӨдҪңж”¶пјҢйҮ‘еғҸйӣ»и·ҢйҖҫеҚҠж №еҒңжқҝпјҢе»ЈйҒ”гҖҒеҸ°йҒ”йӣ»гҖҒйҙ»жө·гҖҒиҒҜзҷјз§‘зӯүйӣ»еӯҗж¬ҠеҖјиӮЎд№ҹиЎЁзҸҫд№ҸеҠӣпјҢиҖҢе…үйҖҡиЁҠж—ҸзҫӨжҢҒзәҢз–ІејұпјҢе…Ёж–°гҖҒиЁҠиҠҜ-KYж…ҳйҒӯи·ҢеҒңпјҢиҒҜдәһгҖҒIET-KYгҖҒе…үиҒ–гҖҒжіўиӢҘеЁҒгҖҒиҸҜжҳҹе…үеҗҢжӯҘдёӢжҢ«гҖӮиЁҳжҶ¶й«”ж—ҸзҫӨжјІи·Ңдә’иҰӢпјҢе®ңйјҺгҖҒе•ҶдёһйӣҷйӣҷдёӢи·ҢйҖҫ4%пјҢе®Үзһ»гҖҒе»Јз©ҺгҖҒеүөиҰӢж”¶й»‘пјҢиҸҜйӮҰйӣ»еүҮеј·жјІйҖҫ5%пјҢж—әе®ҸгҖҒеҚ—дәһз§‘гҖҒеҚҒйҠ“гҖҒзҫӨиҒҜгҖҒеЁҒеүӣйҪҠжҸҡгҖӮ

д»Ҙ29еӨ§йЎһиӮЎиЎЁзҸҫдҫҶзңӢпјҢе…үйӣ»иӮЎдёҠжјІ1.38%иЎЁзҸҫжңҖдҪігҖҒдё»иҰҒдҝӮзҫӨеүөиҪүеј·ж•ҲжҮүпјҢж©ЎиҶ гҖҒйҒӢеӢ•дј‘й–’йЎһиӮЎд№ҹеҲҶеҲҘжңү1.32%гҖҒ1.09%жјІе№…пјҢжјІе№…еҲҶеҲҘеұ…з¬¬дәҢиҲҮз¬¬дёүгҖӮеҸҚи§Җж•ёдҪҚйӣІз«ҜйЎһиӮЎеӣ еҸ—еҲ°еҜҢйӮҰеӘ’иҪүи·ҢеҒңеҪұйҹҝгҖҒж”¶и·Ң7.11%иЎЁзҸҫжңҖе·®пјҢз”ҹжҠҖйҶ«зҷӮгҖҒз¶ иғҪз’°дҝқгҖҒйӣ»и…ҰеҸҠйҖұйӮҠиЁӯеӮҷйЎһиӮЎд№ҹиЎЁзҸҫй»Ҝж·ЎгҖӮ

дёүеӨ§жі•дәәеӢ•еҗ‘ж–№йқўпјҢд»Ҡж—ҘдёүеӨ§жі•дәәеҗҲиЁҲиіЈи¶…353.91е„„е…ғпјӣе…¶дёӯпјҢиҮӘзҮҹе•ҶиіЈи¶…124е„„е…ғпјҢжҠ•дҝЎиІ·и¶…127.88е„„е…ғпјҢеӨ–иіҮеҸҠйҷёиіҮиіЈи¶…357.79е„„е…ғгҖӮ

гҖҠеҸ°иӮЎзӣӨеҫҢгҖӢй–ӢдҪҺиө°дҪҺгҖҒйҮҚж‘”1478й»һпјҢеӨұе®ҲжңҲз·ҡ

еҸ°иӮЎз„Ұй»һпјҡеҸҜжҲҗ(2474.TW)

иіҮж–ҷдҫҶжәҗ- MoneyDJзҗҶиІЎз¶І

news.Title: SpaceX Goes Public at $135 a Share. Is It a Buy at That Price?
news.Content:
SpaceX (NASDAQ: SPCX) stock is set to launch on Friday at $135 per share in what's almost certainly going to be the biggest initial public offering ever. The stock is being heavily marketed through the company's roadshow and its IPO partners, including the underwriting investment banks and several online trading platforms.

If everything goes as planned, many SpaceX employees might become millionaires overnight, and Elon Musk could become the world's first trillionaire. But what about retail investors? At $135 a pop, is this a stock to consider?

Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. Continue В»

Why is SpaceX's IPO price so high?

SpaceX is aiming for a valuation near $2 trillion, and to meet that threshold, it wants to raise $75 billion. That's in addition to what the company is already worth privately. To make that happen, the company is issuing 555,555,555 shares to the public at $135 apiece.

How does it actually work? The company is already highly valued as a private entity. The part going to the public via the IPO represents only about 5% of the company's ownership, and the low supply is likely to drive high demand, which is why investors are willing to pay the high price.

Typically, a company conducts an IPO roadshow to gauge what institutional investors think the stock would be worth on the open market. Management is doing its roadshow to build hype and boost demand, but it determined the $135 sell price early on.

As for the stock already owned by insiders, there are various lockup periods before they can sell. That helps create stability, since people who own shares that were originally worth something like $2 per share might be tempted to sell at the current price, which could flood the market and create an oversupply. According to the Wall Street Journal , pre-IPO SpaceX shares have gained 2,000% over the past few years.

Is this a great opportunity for retail investors?

SpaceX has the retail investor in mind for the IPO, and it has earmarked more than 20% of the shares for retail investors, according to reports. Typical IPOs earmark 5% to 7% of shares directly to retail investors. Five years ago, Musk tweeted about it on what was then Twitter and is now his privately owned X social media app: "Will make sure they get top priority. You can hold me to it," he wrote.

Story Continues

But is this arrangement favorable for retail investors? At $135 per share, using the company's 12-month trailing revenue figures, shares will trade at 92 times sales. That's a hefty premium for a company demonstrating 15% year-over-year revenue growth and a $4.3 billion loss in the 2026 first quarter. IPOs alone add to a company's expenses, including stock-based compensation, and could keep it in the red a bit longer.

The average S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) price-to-sales ratio is 3.7 right now, and even the Nasdaq-100 , which is expected to have a higher average because the index is full of high-growth tech stocks, has an average price-to-sales ratio of 6.7.

Alas, it doesn't look like SpaceX stock is a good buy for retail investors at $135, considering its current financials. However, it's more about the valuation than the price tag. If sales increase, the company becomes profitable, and the valuation metrics become more reasonable, $135 might be a good price in the future.

If you're interested in buying SpaceX stock sooner, you might want to wait a few weeks and see if the price has come down. If you resist a bit longer, IPO prices generally come down even more as lockup periods end and more stock is sold by insiders.

news.Title: еҸ°иӮЎеҚғй»һеӨ§жҙ—зӣӨ дёӯдҝЎ00981DгҖҢжҠ—йңҮпјӢй«ҳзҘЁжҒҜгҖҚжҲҗиіҮйҮ‘йҒҝйўЁжёҜ
news.Content:
зҫҺеңӢз¶“жҝҹж•ёж“ҡеј·еӢҒиҲҮйҖҡиҶЁй»ҸжҖ§е°ҺиҮҙйҷҚжҒҜй җжңҹе»¶еҫҢпјҢеҠ еҠҮе…ЁзҗғйҮ‘иһҚеёӮе ҙеҠҮзғҲйңҮзӣӘпјҢеҸ°иӮЎдәҰйқўиҮЁеҚғй»һеӨ§жҙ—зӣӨзҡ„еҠҮзғҲжіўеӢ•гҖӮжі•дәәжҢҮеҮәпјҢеңЁеҲ©зҺҮз¶ӯжҢҒй«ҳжӘ”зҡ„з’°еўғдёӢпјҢжҠ•иіҮжҖқз¶ӯжҮүз”ұиіҮжң¬еҲ©еҫ—иҪүеҗ‘ж”¶зӣҠдҫҶжәҗпјӣе…¶дёӯпјҢйқһжҠ•иіҮзӯүзҙҡеӮөжҶ‘и—үијғй«ҳзҘЁжҒҜиҲҮдҝЎз”ЁеҲ©е·®дҝ®еҫ©з©әй–“пјҢйҳІзҰҰе„ӘеӢўйЎҜзҸҫгҖӮд»ҘдёӯдҝЎдё»еӢ•йқһжҠ•зӯүеӮө (00981D-TW) зӮәдҫӢпјҢж—ҘеүҚе…¬е‘ҠжңҖж–°й…ҚжҒҜпјҢе№ҙеҢ–й…ҚжҒҜзҺҮз¶ӯжҢҒеңЁ 8% д»ҘдёҠй«ҳж°ҙжә–пјҢд»Ҡ (11) ж—ҘйҖҶеӢўж”¶жјІпјҢеңЁиӮЎеӮөзӣӨеӢўйңҮзӣӘд№ӢйҡӣпјҢжҲҗзӮәиіҮз”ўй…ҚзҪ®зҡ„йҳІзҰҰйҰ–йҒёгҖӮ

зҫҺеңӢжңҖж–°е°ұжҘӯж•ёж“ҡе„Әж–јй җжңҹпјҢеҠ дёҠзҫҺдјҠжҲ°зҲӯе»¶е®•е°ҺиҮҙжІ№еғ№йӣЈд»ҘеӣһиҗҪпјҢж—ҘеүҚзҫҺеңӢе…¬еёғ CPI е№ҙеўһзҺҮжӣҙеӣһеҲ° 4.2% зҡ„й«ҳй»һпјҢеЈ“жҠ‘зҫҺиӮЎеӣһжӘ”пјҢжјІеӨҡзҡ„з§‘жҠҖиӮЎжҲҗйҮҚзҒҪеҚҖгҖӮеҸ°иӮЎд»Ҡж—ҘдәҰеҸ—еҲ°ең°з·Јж”ҝжІ»иҲҮйҖҡиҶЁз–‘ж…®е№Іж“ҫпјҢиҮӘй«ҳжӘ”еӨ§е№…еӣһжӘ”пјҢзӣӨдёӯдёҖеәҰйҮҚжҢ« 1200 й»һпјҢзӣӨеӢўжіўеӢ•жҖҘйҒҪеҠ еҠҮгҖӮ

жі•дәәиЎЁзӨәпјҢеңЁз¶“жҝҹж•ёж“ҡжңӘжҳҺйЎҜиҪүејұгҖҒйҖҡиҶЁд»Қе…·й»ҸжҖ§зҡ„з’°еўғдёӢпјҢиҒҜжә–жңғж”ҝзӯ–и·Ҝеҫ‘зҹӯжңҹйӣЈд»Ҙеҝ«йҖҹиҪүеҗ‘еҜ¬й¬ҶпјҢеёӮе ҙз”ҡиҮідёҚжҺ’йҷӨе№ҙе…§д»ҚжңүеҚҮжҒҜеҸҜиғҪпјҢдҪҝеҲ©зҺҮй«ҳжӘ”еҒңз•ҷжҷӮй–“е»¶й•·зҡ„ж©ҹзҺҮдёҠеҚҮгҖӮ

дё»еӢ•дёӯдҝЎйқһжҠ•зӯүеӮө ETF з¶“зҗҶдәәйҷіжҳұеҪ°жҢҮеҮәпјҢйҡЁи‘— FOMC жңғиӯ°еҚіе°Үзҷ»е ҙпјҢеёӮе ҙе°ҮжҢҒзәҢй—ңжіЁиҒҜжә–жңғе°ҚеҲ©зҺҮиҲҮйҖҡиҶЁзҡ„жңҖж–°жҢҮеј•гҖӮжғҹеңЁж”ҝзӯ–дёҚзўәе®ҡжҖ§еҚҮжә«еҸҠиіҮйҮ‘йқўеҒҸз·ҠеҪұйҹҝдёӢпјҢеӮөеёӮиІ·зӣӨиҪүи¶Ёдҝқе®ҲпјҢзҹӯз·ҡж®–еҲ©зҺҮд»ҚжңүдёҠиЎҢз©әй–“пјҢж•ҙй«”йҮ‘иһҚеёӮе ҙй җжңҹе»¶зәҢйңҮзӣӘж јеұҖпјҢдҪҝз•¶еүҚиіҮз”ўй…ҚзҪ®жӣҙйЎҜйҮҚиҰҒгҖӮ

еңЁжӯӨз’°еўғдёӢпјҢе…·еӮҷж”¶зӣҠж”Ҝж’җзҡ„еӮөеҲёиіҮз”ўзӣёе°Қеұ•зҸҫжҠ—йңҮе„ӘеӢўгҖӮйҷіжҳұеҪ°еҲҶжһҗпјҢйқһжҠ•иіҮзӯүзҙҡеӮөеӣ ж“Ғжңүијғй«ҳзҘЁжҒҜж”¶е…ҘпјҢеңЁеҲ©зҺҮз¶ӯжҢҒй«ҳжӘ”жңҹй–“пјҢеҸҜжҸҗдҫӣијғдҪізҡ„ж”¶зӣҠз·©иЎқпјӣеҗҢжҷӮдјҒжҘӯеҹәжң¬йқўд»Қз©©еҒҘпјҢйҒ•зҙ„йўЁйҡӘжңӘиҰӢжҳҺйЎҜеҚҮжә«пјҢдҪҝдҝЎз”ЁеҲ©е·®е…·еӮҷжҢҒзәҢдҝ®еҫ©з©әй–“пјҢжңүеҠ©жҸҗеҚҮж•ҙй«”жҠ•иіҮе ұй…¬иЎЁзҸҫпјҢжңүжңӣжҢҒзәҢеҗёеј•йҒҝйҡӘиіҮйҮ‘йқ’зқһгҖӮ

д»Ҙ 00981D зӮәдҫӢпјҢд»Ҡж—ҘеңЁеҸ°иӮЎзӣӨдёӯеӨ§е№…дҝ®жӯЈд№ӢйҡӣпјҢиЎЁзҸҫзӣёе°ҚжҠ—и·ҢпјҢз”ҡиҮіеұ•зҸҫйҖҶеӢўиө°еј·ж…ӢеӢўпјҢеҮёйЎҜе…¶еңЁиіҮз”ўй…ҚзҪ®дёӯзҡ„йҳІзҰҰеҠҹиғҪгҖӮи©ІеҹәйҮ‘жҺЎеҸ–дё»еӢ•йҒёеӮөзӯ–з•ҘпјҢиҒҡз„ҰжҲҗзҶҹеёӮе ҙе„ӘиіӘйқһжҠ•иіҮзӯүзҙҡеӮөпјҢдёҰйҖҸйҒҺеӢ•ж…ӢиӘҝж•ҙз”ўжҘӯиҲҮдҝЎз”Ёй…ҚзҪ®пјҢжҲҗеҠҹеңЁеёӮе ҙжіўеӢ•жҷӮе…јйЎ§йўЁйҡӘжҺ§з®ЎиҲҮж”¶зӣҠж©ҹжңғгҖӮ00981D ж—ҘеүҚд№ҹе…¬е‘ҠжңҖж–°й…ҚжҒҜиіҮиЁҠпјҢжҜҸе–®дҪҚж“¬й…Қзҷј 0.07 е…ғпјҢжҸӣз®—е№ҙеҢ–й…ҚжҒҜзҺҮд»Қз¶ӯжҢҒ 8% д»ҘдёҠж°ҙжә–гҖӮ

еұ•жңӣжңӘдҫҶпјҢйҷіжҳұеҪ°еј·иӘҝпјҢеңЁеҲ©зҺҮж”ҝзӯ–д»ҚеҒҸеҜ©ж…ҺгҖҒиіҮйҮ‘з’°еўғеҲҶеҢ–зҡ„иғҢжҷҜдёӢпјҢеёӮе ҙжҠ•иіҮе°Үз”ұйҒҺеҺ»и‘—йҮҚгҖҢиіҮжң¬еҲ©еҫ—гҖҚпјҢйҖҗжӯҘиҪүеҗ‘йҮҚиҰ–гҖҢж”¶зӣҠдҫҶжәҗгҖҚгҖӮйқһжҠ•иіҮзӯүзҙҡеӮөжҶ‘и—үијғй«ҳзҘЁжҒҜе„ӘеӢўиҲҮдҝЎз”ЁеҲ©е·®дҝ®еҫ©з©әй–“пјҢеҫҢеёӮзңӢеҘҪгҖӮиҖҢ 00981D йҖҸйҒҺдё»еӢ•ејҸз®ЎзҗҶж©ҹеҲ¶пјҢеҸҜйқҲжҙ»еӣ жҮүеёӮе ҙи®ҠеҢ–пјҢеҠӣжұӮеңЁжҺ§еҲ¶жіўеӢ•зҡ„еҗҢжҷӮжҸҗеҚҮж”¶зӣҠжҪӣеҠӣпјҢйҒ©еҗҲдҪңзӮәжҠ•иіҮзө„еҗҲдёӯе…јйЎ§йҳІзҰҰиҲҮж”¶зӣҠзҡ„ж ёеҝғй…ҚзҪ®гҖӮ

news.Title: Meta Starts Unwinding Manus Deal by Splitting Operations, Data
news.Content:
(Bloomberg) -- Meta Platforms Inc. has completed an operational split from Manus and halted data sharing between the two companies, taking a pivotal step toward unwinding a $2 billion acquisition opposed by Beijing.

Most Read from Bloomberg

House Republican Says HegsethвҖҷs D-Day Remarks вҖҳInappropriateвҖҷ

Xbox Plans Significant Layoffs as New CEO Plans Overhaul

US Strikes Iran in Trump Escalation Over Stalled Peace Talks

Tech Stocks Sink as Oil Jumps on US-Iran Jitters: Markets Wrap

H-1B Visa Rules Have Changed Again. What to Know

The owner of Facebook and Instagram has effectively erected a firewall between itself and the Chinese-founded agentic AI service, people familiar with the matter said. Meta has barred Manus and its staff from accessing the US companyвҖҷs internal data systems since the start of the month, the people said. Meta employees, in turn, can no longer use Manus tools for internal projects, the people said, asking to remain anonymous to discuss private decisions.

The ringfencing comes as ManusвҖҷs founders explore options to fulfill BeijingвҖҷs demand to undo the deal, including by raising about $1 billion to fund a buyback. Meta is вҖңsunsettingвҖқ Manus, according to an internal memo viewed by Bloomberg News. Staff were told to migrate existing Manus projects onto MetaвҖҷs systems and not to start new work on the AI platform, the memo showed. Together, the actions taken to separate the two operations mark another step toward eventually hiving off Manus, the people said.

Once celebrated as a blueprint for Chinese AI startups keen to set foot on a global stage, MetaвҖҷs landmark acquisition of Manus quickly drew criticism for handing over key technology to a geopolitical rival, triggering a months-long probe involving tech export controls. Chinese regulators in April demanded the deal be unwound, triggering the intricate process of dismantling a completed transaction.

Manus, once hailed as a breakthrough that would challenge Silicon ValleyвҖҷs dominance, is turning into a cautionary tale for Chinese entrepreneurs.

The founders got their start in China but relocated their headquarters and key staff to Singapore in 2025. When the deal was announced in December, it wasnвҖҷt clear whether Beijing would exert authority over a transaction that technically took place beyond its borders.

ManusвҖҷs three founders вҖ” Xiao Hong, Ji Yichao, and Zhang Tao вҖ” have begun discussions about raising money to fund a buyback at a valuation that would at least match the $2 billion Meta paid, Bloomberg News reported last month.

ItвҖҷs unclear if discussions around a deal have advanced significantly. Manus staff have moved into Meta offices in Singapore, while investors including Tencent Holdings Ltd., ZhenFund and HSG have received their proceeds from MetaвҖҷs acquisition, people familiar with the matter said previously.

Story Continues

Since BeijingвҖҷs April decision, Manus has continued to add new features to its service.

ItвҖҷs integrated data from Similarweb and added functionality from e-commerce software maker Shopify, according to announcements on ManusвҖҷs website. Its users are still given the option to access MetaвҖҷs Ads Manager service as of this week, and connect with Instagram along with other services such as Gmail and coding platform GitHub.

--With assistance from Zheping Huang and Newley Purnell.

news.Title: еӨ–иіҮзәҢиіЈ358е„„пјҒ еҸ°иӮЎдёҠжј”еҚғй»һеӨ§йҖҶиҪү з¶Із¬‘пјҡжІ’иІЁдәҶ
news.Content:
еҸ°иӮЎд»ҠпјҲ11пјүж—Ҙж—©зӣӨдёҖеәҰжҡҙи·Ңи¶…йҒҺ1200й»һпјҢжңҖдҪҺдёӢжҺў42006й»һпјҢйҖјиҝ‘42000й»һж•ҙж•ёй—ңеҚЎгҖӮдёҚйҒҺйҡЁи‘—дҪҺжҺҘиІ·зӣӨеј·еӢўйҖІе ҙпјҢжҢҮж•ёзөӮе ҙж”¶еңЁ43149й»һпјҢдёӢи·Ң76й»һпјҢз•ҷдёӢйҖҫеҚғй»һй•·дёӢеҪұз·ҡпјҢдёүеӨ§жі•дәәеүҮжҳҜеҗҲиЁҲиіЈи¶…124е„„е…ғгҖӮ

ж №ж“ҡдёүеӨ§жі•дәәиІ·иіЈи¶…зөұиЁҲпјҢд»Ҡж—ҘеҗҲиЁҲиіЈи¶…353.91е„„е…ғпјҢе…¶дёӯеӨ–иіҮжҢҒзәҢз«ҷеңЁиіЈж–№пјҢе–®ж—ҘиіЈи¶…357.79е„„е…ғпјҢзӮәзӣӨйқўдё»иҰҒиіЈеЈ“дҫҶжәҗпјӣжҠ•дҝЎеүҮжҢҒзәҢжү®жј”иӯ·зӣӨиҰҒи§’пјҢиІ·и¶…127.88е„„е…ғпјҢиҮӘзҮҹе•ҶеҗҲиЁҲиіЈи¶…124е„„е…ғгҖӮ

йӣ–з„¶еӨ–иіҮд»Қз¶ӯжҢҒиіЈи¶…пјҢдҪҶзӣёијғе…ҲеүҚеӢ•иј’иҝ‘еҚғе„„е…ғзҡ„жҸҗж¬ҫиҰҸжЁЎпјҢд»Ҡж—ҘиіЈи¶…йҮ‘йЎҚжҳҺйЎҜзё®жёӣпјҢд№ҹи®“еёӮе ҙй–Ӣе§ӢеҮәзҸҫгҖҢиіЈеЈ“йҲҚеҢ–гҖҚзҡ„иЁҺи«–иҒІжөӘгҖӮPTTиӮЎжқҝз¶ІеҸӢе°Қж–јеӨ–иіҮеӢ•еҗ‘иӯ°и«–зҙӣзҙӣпјҢдёҚе°‘дәәзҷјзҸҫеӨ–иіҮиіЈи¶…йҮ‘йЎҚдҪҺж–јй җжңҹпјҢзӣҙе‘јгҖҢиӘ°еңЁиіЈпјҹгҖҚгҖҒгҖҢиіЈзҡ„и®Ҡе°‘дәҶгҖҚгҖҒгҖҢеӨ–иіҮеү©300еӨҡе„„пјҢиҰҒй–Ӣе§ӢеҸҚиҪүдәҶе—ҺгҖҚгҖҒгҖҢе°Ҹе…’иіЈдёҚеӢ•дәҶпјҢеҸҚж”»иҷҹи§’йҹҝиө·гҖҚгҖӮд№ҹжңүдёҚе°‘з¶ІеҸӢиӘҚзӮәпјҢд»Ҡж—ҘзӣӨдёӯжҖҘи·ҢеҫҢзҡ„еј·еӢўеҸҚеҪҲпјҢеҸҜиғҪд»ЈиЎЁеёӮе ҙжүҝжҺҘеҠӣйҒ“зӣёз•¶еј·еӢҒпјҢгҖҢжІ’иІЁеҸҜд»ҘиіЈдәҶеҗјпјҢеҶҚиіЈе•ҠгҖҚгҖҒгҖҢе°Ҹе…’жІ’иІЁдәҶгҖҚгҖҒгҖҢеӨ–иіҮжІ’еҠӣж°ЈиіЈдәҶгҖҚгҖҒгҖҢдёҚж•ўиіЈдәҶе–”гҖҚгҖҒгҖҢиіЈдёҚеӢ•дәҶпјҢзңӢдҫҶиҰҒй–Ӣе§ӢиІ·еӣһгҖҚгҖӮеҸҰдёҖжҙҫеүҮиӘҚзӮәпјҢе–®зңӢиІ·иіЈи¶…ж•ёеӯ—жңӘеҝ…иғҪе®Ңж•ҙеҸҚжҳ зӣӨдёӯж“ҚдҪңпјҢжңүз¶ІеҸӢеҲҶжһҗжҢҮеҮәпјҢеӨ–иіҮеҸҜиғҪжҳҜеңЁй–ӢзӣӨжҒҗж…Ңж®әзӣӨжҷӮеӨ§иҲүиіЈеҮәпјҢйҡЁеҫҢеҸҲйҖўдҪҺеӣһиЈңпјҢеӣ жӯӨжңҖзөӮиіЈи¶…йҮ‘йЎҚжүҚеғ…зҙ„358е„„е…ғпјҢгҖҢи·Ң1200й»һжҷӮеҸҜиғҪеӨ§иіЈпјҢеҫҢйқўеҸҲиӘҚйҢҜиІ·еӣһдҫҶгҖҚгҖҒгҖҢе…Ҳз Қ1000е„„еҫҢйқўиЈң700е„„гҖҚгҖҒгҖҢд»ҠеӨ©еҫһеә•йғЁжӢү1000й»һпјҢеҶҚзңӢзңӢеӨ–иіҮеҲ°еә•еңЁе“ӘиЈЎиІ·гҖҚгҖӮ

жӯӨеӨ–пјҢжҠ•дҝЎжҢҒзәҢз«ҷеңЁиІ·ж–№д№ҹзҚІеҫ—дёҚе°‘жҠ•иіҮдәәиӮҜе®ҡпјҢгҖҢжҠ•дҝЎжҲ‘еӨ§е“ҘгҖҚгҖҒгҖҢжҠ•дҝЎд№ҹеӨӘзөҰеҠӣдәҶеҗ§гҖҚгҖҒгҖҢеӨ§еӘҪеҸҲиҙҸдәҶгҖҚгҖҒгҖҢйғҪиў«еӨ§еӘҪиІ·е…үдәҶгҖҚпјҢиӘҚзӮәе…§иіҮиІ·зӣӨжҲҗзӮәж”Ҝж’җеҸ°иӮЎзҡ„йҮҚиҰҒеҠӣйҮҸгҖӮ

ж•ҙй«”иҖҢиЁҖпјҢйӣ–з„¶еӨ–иіҮд»Қе»¶зәҢиіЈи¶…ж…ӢеӢўпјҢдҪҶеңЁеҸ°иӮЎзӣӨдёӯйҮҚжҢ«йҖҫ1200й»һеҫҢд»ҚиғҪеӨ§е№…ж”¶ж–Ӯи·ҢеӢўпјҢйЎҜзӨәеёӮе ҙдҪҺжӘ”жүҝжҺҘж„ҸйЎҳдёҚејұгҖӮйҡЁи‘—еӨ–иіҮиіЈи¶…иҰҸжЁЎзё®е°ҸпјҢеҠ дёҠжҠ•дҝЎжҢҒзәҢеҠ зўјпјҢеҫҢзәҢжҳҜеҗҰжҲҗзӮәзҹӯз·ҡжӯўи·Ңз”ҡиҮіеҸҚж”»иЁҠиҷҹпјҢд»ҚжҳҜеёӮе ҙй—ңжіЁз„Ұй»һгҖӮ

пјҲе°ҒйқўзӨәж„Ҹең–пјҸAIз”ҹжҲҗпјү

гҖҗ жң¬ж–Ү з”ұ жқұжЈ®иІЎз¶“ жҸҗдҫӣпјҢжңӘз¶“жҺҲж¬ҠпјҢи«ӢеӢҝиҪүијүпјҒгҖ‘

news.Title: Musk to speak at chip tool giant ASML event ahead of SpaceX IPO
news.Content:
By Toby Sterling

AMSTERDAM, June 11 (Reuters) - Elon Musk is expected to appear virtually at an event by computer chip вҖҢequipment giant ASML on Thursday to discuss Terafab, his вҖҢplan for a massive chipmaking plant to supply Tesla and IPO-bound SpaceX.

The pricing of вҖӢthe initial public offering of Musk's SpaceX, potentially the largest ever, is expected later on Thursday.

"Musk will share his vision on AI, robotics, space, and semiconductor manufacturing," an ASML spokesperson said in a statement.

ASML вҖҢdescribes its annual one-day вҒ technology conference as an "internal event for employees only" and would not specify when Musk will speak. The вҒ conference ends around 5 p.m. (1500 GMT).

ASML CEO Christophe Fouquet last month confirmed he has had discussions with Musk over Terafab, potentially a large new вҖӢcustomer вҖӢfor ASML, Europe's biggest company by вҖӢmarket valuation. ASML dominates вҖҢthe market for lithography tools, machines that help "print" the tiny circuitry of chips.

Musk has said SpaceX will cooperate with Intel on the Terafab project, and it will eventually supply chips for SpaceX and for Tesla's Optimus robots.

In March, Musk laid out plans for an вҖҢinitial phase in which a development вҖӢplant in Texas will make lithography masks - вҖӢwhich contain the pattern for вҖӢchips "printed" by ASML tools - to quickly prototype both logic and вҖҢmemory chips under the same вҖӢroof.

"Musk and his вҖӢteam are becoming part of the broader semiconductor ecosystem, and many companies, including ASML, will collaborate on this initiative," ASML вҖӢsaid.

In a June 6 вҖҢstatement on X, Musk said ASML is "arguably the greatest вҖӢcompany in Europe" and it should be "treasured and supported."

(Reporting вҖӢby Toby SterlingEditing by Tomasz Janowski)

ж №ж“ҡж‘©ж №еҸІдё№еҲ©жңҖж–°зҷјеёғзҡ„гҖҢеӨ§дёӯиҸҜеҚҠе°Һй«”гҖҚз ”з©¶е ұе‘ҠжҢҮеҮәпјҢжҠ•иіҮдәәиҝ‘жңҹеӣ зӮәж“”жҶӮCPOпјҲе…ұе°ҒиЈқе…үеӯёпјүзҡ„е°Һе…ҘйҖІеәҰеҮәзҸҫйҒһе»¶пјҢе°ҺиҮҙзӣёй—ңжҰӮеҝөиӮЎиӮЎеғ№еҮәзҸҫйҖІдёҖжӯҘзҡ„дҝ®жӯЈ гҖӮеӨ§ж‘©еңЁе ұе‘ҠдёӯзӣҙиЁҖпјҢзӣ®еүҚжҠ•иіҮдәәжҷ®йҒҚй җжңҹ2027е№ҙе…үеӯёеј•ж“Һзҡ„еўһй•·еҸҜд»ҘйҒ”еҲ°2000иҗ¬иҮі3000иҗ¬йЎҶзҡ„иҰҸжЁЎпјҢдҪҶж №ж“ҡд»–еҖ‘йҮҚж–°жӘўиҰ–дҫӣжҮүйҸҲеҫҢзҡ„еҜҰйҡӣе»әжЁЎдј°з®—пјҢ2027е№ҙе…үеӯёеј•ж“Һзҡ„ж•ҙй«”еҮәиІЁйҮҸпјҢеҜҰйҡӣдёҠеҸҜиғҪиҗҪеңЁ600иҗ¬иҮі700иҗ¬йЎҶе·ҰеҸі гҖӮйҖҷзЁ®й җжңҹдёҠзҡ„иҗҪе·®пјҢеңЁзҹӯжңҹе…§еӢўеҝ…жңғеЈ“жҠ‘CPOжҰӮеҝөиӮЎзҡ„еёӮе ҙжғ…з·’пјҢдҪҶд№ҹзөҰдәҶеёӮе ҙдёҖеҖӢйҮҚж–°иӘҝж•ҙи…іжӯҘгҖҒйҮҚиЁӯй җжңҹзҡ„ж©ҹжңғ гҖӮ

й»ғйҮ‘жӢҗй»һй җжңҹиҗҪеңЁгҖҢйҖҷжҷӮй–“гҖҚ

йқўе°Қз”ўжҘӯзөҗж§Ӣзҡ„иҪүи®ҠпјҢеӨ§ж‘©й җжё¬2026е№ҙиҮі2028е№ҙй–“пјҢеёӮе ҙдёҰдёҚжңғз”ұе–®дёҖжҠҖиЎ“еЈҹж–·пјҢиҖҢжҳҜдёҖеҖӢзҷҫе®¶зҲӯйіҙзҡ„е…ұеӯҳзӢҖж…Ӣ гҖӮеӨ§ж‘©еңЁе ұе‘ҠдёӯжҢҮеҮәпјҢеңЁйҖҷж®өжңҹй–“е…§пјҢеӮізөұзҡ„еҸҜжҸ’жӢ”е…үж”¶зҷјеҷЁгҖҒе…үеӯёжҠҖиЎ“пјҲеҰӮCPO/NPOпјүд»ҘеҸҠйҠ…зәңи§Јжұәж–№жЎҲе°Үжңғз¶ӯжҢҒе’Ңи«§е…ұеӯҳзҡ„зҜҖеҘҸ гҖӮеӨ§ж‘©еј·иӘҝCPOе°Үеҫһ2028е№ҙй–Ӣе§ӢиҝҺдҫҶзңҹжӯЈзҡ„и“¬еӢғзҷјеұ•пјҢйҖҷйӣ–з„¶жӢүй•·дәҶзӯүеҫ…жңҹпјҢеҚ»д№ҹзӮәй•·з·ҡзҡ„жҠ•иіҮиҖ…ж”ҜжҢҒдәҶдёҖжўқжӣҙе…·е»¶зәҢжҖ§зҡ„еӮ¬еҢ–еҠ‘и·Ҝеҫ‘ гҖӮ

еӨ§ж‘©й»һеҗҚеҸ°зі»7дё»еҠӣ

е„ҳз®Ўиҝ‘жңҹзҡ„еёӮе ҙдҝ®жӯЈи®“ж°Јж°ӣйЎҜеҫ—жңүдәӣеҶ·жё…пјҢдҪҶеӨ§ж‘©е°Қж•ҙй«”з”ўжҘӯзҡ„й•·з·ҡзңӢжі•дҫқиҲҠжІ’жңүеӢ•жҗ– гҖӮеӨ§ж‘©еј·иӘҝпјҡгҖҢжҲ‘еҖ‘е°Қй—ңйҚөзҡ„CPOжҺЁеӢ•иҖ…з¶ӯжҢҒгҖҺеҠ зўјгҖҸпјҲOverweightпјүи©•зӯүпјҢеӣ зӮәCPOзҡ„й•·жңҹж•…дәӢдҫқиҲҠжІ’жңүж”№и®ҠгҖӮгҖҚеңЁеҸ°иӮЎж–№йқўпјҢиў«еҲҶжһҗеё«зҫӨзү№еҲҘй»һеҗҚзңӢеҘҪзҡ„ж ёеҝғдҫӣжҮүйҸҲжҲҗе“ЎеҢ…жӢ¬пјҡеҸ°з©Қйӣ»гҖҒж—ҘжңҲе…үжҠ•жҺ§гҖҒдёҠи©®гҖҒиҗ¬жҪӨгҖҒж—әзҹҪгҖҒйҙ»еӢҒд»ҘеҸҠз©Һеҙҙ гҖӮеӨ§ж‘©йҖҸйңІпјҢеғҸжҳҜдёҠи©®зҡ„CPOзӣёй—ңзҮҹж”¶й җиЁҲеңЁз¬¬дёүеӯЈжң«е°ұжңғй–Ӣе§ӢзңӢеҲ°ж”ҖеҚҮпјҢеңЁжӯӨд№ӢеүҚеӣ зӮәжӯЈиҷ•ж–јйҮҸз”ўеүҚзҡ„жә–еӮҷйҡҺж®өпјҢзҮҹж”¶иЎЁзҸҫз¶ӯжҢҒжә«еҗһжҳҜжӯЈеёёзҸҫиұЎ гҖӮ

news.Title: Apple Will Make Billions From AI in These 2 Ways. Is It Time to Buy the Stock?
news.Content:
Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL) unveiled its much-anticipated Siri update at its recent Worldwide Developers Conference, and it's a big deal for the company.

The new Siri AI, as Apple is calling it, can remember your past conversations, search through users' photos, messages, and email, and understand what you're looking at on your screen. It also offers a more conversational interface ( like ChatGPT ).

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue В»

Investors had a mixed reaction to the news: Apple shares climbed on the day of the launch, then dropped again the next day.

But despite the market's uncertainty, there are two important ways Apple is setting itself up to make billions of dollars from Siri AI. And this upgrade may be important enough to justify investors picking up some Apple stock right now.

A new iPhone upgrade supercycle is likely on the way

The first way Apple will likely make billions of dollars from Siri AI is by only making the new features available for more recent models -- the ones that already support its previous Apple Intelligence tools. Those start with the iPhone 15 Pro, iPhone 15 Pro Max, as well as all iPhone 16s and 17s, the iPhone Air, and upcoming models.

Because iPhone models from before 2023 won't be compatible with the new Siri AI, many people may take this as their cue to upgrade. This is a long-standing play that Apple runs from its playbook, and it typically works well.

Some analysts expect Apple's improved artificial intelligence offerings to create a supercycle that eventually tips the scales for a large fraction of the current 1.5 billion iPhone users worldwide to buy the latest models. Apple generated more than $209 billion in iPhone sales in fiscal 2025, so fractionally more users than average upgrading their devices over the next several years could bring in tens of billions of dollars in additional sales.

Siri AI could cause services revenue to surge

Apple is giving away most of its AI tools to its users for free, but it will set daily usage limits on some of their most advanced capabilities, such as image generation. This is typical in the AI services space, and it's creating a new revenue opportunity for Apple.

If users want to push their daily usage higher, they'll need to have an iCloud+ subscription. The company has said "most subscriptions" will include expanded Siri AI usage, which likely means users who want it will have to spring for something beyond the lowest iCloud+ tier, which costs just $1 per month. This could be the first step toward Apple transitioning iCloud from primarily a data-storage service to more of an AI service add-on.

Story Continues

And it could eventually be a very lucrative move. Wedbush analyst Dan Ives believes AI services could eventually add $15 billion to $20 billion annually to Apple's services revenue. That's an especially notable prediction considering that Apple's services revenue was $31 billion in its most recent quarter.

Why owning Apple could still be a smart move

I've criticized Apple in the past for fumbling its initial forays into AI. But I've also come to believe that, overall, the company is making strategic moves in artificial intelligence that could benefit it for years to come.

Apple typically takes a slow-and-steady approach to new services and technologies, and it's sticking to that tried-and-true strategy here. If an iPhone upgrade supercycle kicks in over the next few years because users want to get their hands on Siri AI, then sales of Apple's cash cow product will surge.

What's more, I think Apple is still just beginning to determine how best to benefit from its AI services offerings. I wouldn't be surprised to see iCloud+ evolve in the next couple of years to include even more AI services and different tiers.

I believe Apple occupies a unique position in the tech space right now because of its hardware dominance. While many of Apple's peers are racing to create the best AI model and spending hundreds of billions of dollars on data centers, Apple continues to benefit from selling devices with high profit margins.

All of the above is enough to convince me to continue holding onto my Apple shares. If you don't own Apple stock right now, I think opening a position could be a smart move, though I wouldn't expect the types of phenomenal gains that some AI-first companies are experiencing. Rather, buy Apple if you're looking for a more steady approach to the current AI landscape.

news.Title: Nvidia Just Announced a Potential Windfall for Shareholders
news.Content:
There's no question that Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) has been a successful investment for shareholders. The company's graphics processing units (GPUs) still dominate gaming with a 95% share of the market. Nvidia didn't stop there, adapting its processors for all manner of technology, including cryptocurrency, autonomous driving systems, robotics, cloud computing, artificial intelligence (AI), and more.

It was the company's foray into AI that sent the stock on a blistering run, as GPUs proved equally adept at processing AI workloads. Since the advent of AI in early 2023, the stock has gained 1,330% (as of this writing), driving its market cap to $5 trillion and making it the world's most valuable company by a comfortable margin.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue В»

As impressive as its returns have been, there could be much more to come, as Nvidia announced plans that will be extremely lucrative for current and future shareholders.

The dominant force in AI

Before we address the potential windfall, it's helpful to understand the magnitude of Nvidia's success, which continues to accelerate. For its fiscal 2027 first quarter (ended April 26), the company generated record revenue that soared 85% year over year to $81.6 billion, while also accelerating 20% quarter over quarter. Nvidia's gross profit margin remains near record levels at 74.9%. This fueled adjusted earnings per share (EPS) that surged 140% to $1.87.

Make no mistake, it was AI that powered the results, as Nvidia's data center revenue rocketed 92% year over year and 21% quarter over quarter to $75.2 billion, driven by unrelenting demand for the company's Blackwell and Vera Rubin AI chips and accessories. By some accounts, the company controls between 85% and 92% of the data center GPU market.

Management expects Nvidia's accelerating growth to continue. The company is guiding for Q2 revenue to grow 95% to $91 billion.

Perhaps most telling is the company's growing cash generation. Nvidia delivered operating cash flow of $50.3 billion and free cash flow of $48.5 billion. That came despite heavy research and development spending, which grew 58% to $6.3 billion. Nvidia also has a rock-solid balance sheet with $80.5 billion in cash and marketable debt and equity securities, and just $12.3 billion in debt and operating leases.

Story Continues

What to do with all that money?

Nvidia has high-class problems and has obviously been struggling to find new ways to put all that money to good use. The company has been buying stakes in start-ups and taking equity positions in a number of publicly traded AI companies, investing more than $40 billion this year alone. Management has also been ramping up its shareholder returns, spending a record $20 billion in share repurchases and dividends in Q1. Yet its cash pile keeps growing.

The chipmaker then made the eye-popping move of increasing its quarterly dividend 25-fold, from $0.01 to $0.25 per share, payable on June 26 to shareholders of record as of June 4. That puts its dividend yield at roughly 0.5%, suggesting there's still plenty more where that came from.

At Nvidia's GPU Technology Conference (GTC) in Taipei earlier this month, CEO Jensen Huang dropped the mic when he unveiled how the company will spend its growing pile of cash. He said Nvidia plans to return:

50% or more of free cash flow to our shareholders this year, next year, and beyond.

Let's do the math. Wall Street expects Nvidia to generate profits of $217 billion this year and roughly $307 billion next year. Applying the company's current cash flow margin of 42% results in free cash flow of $91 billion and $129 billion, respectively. At 50%, that suggests Nvidia could disperse a total of $110 billion to shareholders over the next two years.

To be clear, those are estimates based on investor expectations, so the actual amount will vary. Returns of that magnitude could significantly reduce the outstanding share count through buybacks or supercharge the dividend through annual increases.

Either eventuality would be a boon to shareholders. And at 22 times forward earnings, Nvidia stock is a bargain.

news.Title: Prediction: Solana Will Outperform Bitcoin Over the Next 3 Years
news.Content: When cryptocurrencies first really took off in 2020 and 2021, the hunt was on for the next cryptocurrency to explode . The frenzy faded, and only a few well-known altcoins have outperformed Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) in the past three years. Solana (CRYPTO: SOL) is one of them, increasing by over 250% while Bitcoin has gained almost 140%. Will AI create ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Solana (SOL) has outperformed Bitcoin (BTC) over the past three years, with SOL increasing by over 250% compared to BTC's 140%.
вҖў Despite recent price drops for both cryptocurrencies due to inflation and geopolitical uncertainty, Solana is seen as having better long-term potential.
вҖў Solana's smart contract capabilities and low transaction fees position it favorably for mainstream financial use and AI agent transactions.
вҖў The article suggests Solana could capture more market share than Bitcoin in areas like stablecoin settlement and AI agent payments.
вҖў With a market capitalization significantly smaller than Bitcoin's, Solana is predicted to outperform Bitcoin over the next three years.
news.Keywords: Cryptocurrency, Altcoin, Bitcoin, Solana, AI Agents

news.Title: еҸ°иӮЎ6жңҲдёҠжј”еӨ§жҖ’зҘһ 7жӘ”й«ҳиӮЎжҒҜETFиҖҒзҘһеңЁеңЁ
news.Content:
еҸ—дёӯжқұең°з·Јж”ҝжІ»йўЁйҡӘеҶҚиө·еҪұйҹҝпјҢеҸ°иӮЎ 10 ж—ҘеӨ§и·Ң 1478.9 й»һпјҢеүөдёӢеҸІдёҠе–®ж—Ҙз¬¬е…ӯеӨ§и·Ңй»һзҙҖйҢ„пјҢд»Ҡ (11) ж—ҘзӣӨдёӯй«ҳдҪҺжҢҜзӣӘйҖҫеҚғй»һпјҢи®“жҠ•иіҮдәәеҝғж…Ңж…ҢгҖӮ6 жңҲд»ҘдҫҶйҮ‘иһҚеёӮе ҙдёҚе№ійқңпјҢеҸ°иӮЎд№ҹдёҠжј”еӨ§жҖ’зҘһпјҢзөұиЁҲеӨ§зӣӨ 6 жңҲд»ҘдҫҶе·ІдёӢи·Ң 3.37%пјҢеҸҚи§Җдәәж°Јжҡўж—әзҡ„еҸ°иӮЎ ETF еҗҢжңҹй–“иЎЁзҸҫпјҢз¶ӯжҢҒжӯЈе ұй…¬зҡ„жңү 24 жӘ”пјҢе…¶дёӯеғ№йҮҸйҪҠжҸҡзҡ„еүҚеҚҒжӘ”дёӯпјҢйҷӨдәҶ 3 жӘ”еҸҚ 1 еӨ–пјҢе…¶д»– 7 жӘ”еҢ…жӢ¬зҫӨзӣҠеҸ°зҒЈзІҫйҒёй«ҳжҒҜ (00919-TW)гҖҒеңӢжі°ж°ёзәҢй«ҳиӮЎжҒҜ (00878-TW)гҖҒе…ғеӨ§еҸ°зҒЈеғ№еҖјй«ҳжҒҜ (00940-TW) зӯүпјҢжё…дёҖиүІйғҪжҳҜеҸ°иӮЎй«ҳжҒҜ ETFпјҢйЎҜзӨәеңЁеӨ§зӣӨйңҮзӣӘд№ӢйҡӣпјҢиіҮйҮ‘ж№§еҗ‘иғҪжңүй«ҳжҒҜж”¶дҝқиӯ·зҡ„еҸ°иӮЎ ETFгҖӮ

и§ҖеҜҹ 6 жңҲд»ҘдҫҶ 10 жӘ”еғ№йҮҸйҪҠжҸҡзҡ„еҸ°иӮЎ ETFпјҢзҫӨзӣҠ 00919 ж—ҘжҲҗдәӨеқҮйҮҸзӮә 19.3 иҗ¬ејөпјҢзӮәеҚҒжӘ”дёӯзҡ„жңҖдҪідәәж°ЈзҺӢпјҢеҸҚеҗ‘ ETF 00632R ж—ҘеқҮйҮҸ 18.6 иҗ¬ејөеұ…ж¬ЎпјҢиҖҢ 00918гҖҒ00878 ж—ҘеқҮйҮҸдәҰи¶…йҒҺ 10 иҗ¬ејөгҖӮ

ж“ҡдәҶи§ЈпјҢ00919 з¬¬ 13 ж¬Ўй…ҚжҒҜйҮ‘йЎҚзӮә 1 е…ғпјҢжҳҜйҖЈзәҢз¬¬дәҢеӯЈеүөж–°й«ҳпјҢй җдј°е№ҙеҢ–й…ҚжҒҜзҺҮзӮәйҖЈзәҢ 13 еӯЈз¶ӯжҢҒ 10% д»ҘдёҠпјҢе»¶зәҢдәҶй«ҳеҸҲз©©зҡ„й…ҚжҒҜж”ҝзӯ–пјҢд»Ҡе№ҙдҫҶжӣҙжҳҜдёҠжјІиҝ‘еӣӣжҲҗпјҢжҒҜеҲ©йӣҷж”¶жңҖеҸҜжңҹпјҢи®“иЁұеӨҡжҠ•иіҮдәәеҚЎдҪҚжҒҜеҲ©йӣҷж”¶жҠ•иіҮеҘ‘ж©ҹгҖӮ00919 жң¬ж¬ЎйҷӨжҒҜж—ҘзӮә 2026 е№ҙ 6 жңҲ 16 ж—ҘпјҢеҸғиҲҮйҷӨжҒҜжңҖеҫҢиІ·йҖІж—ҘзӮә 2026 е№ҙ 6 жңҲ 15 ж—ҘгҖӮ

зҫӨзӣҠжҠ•дҝЎеҸ°иӮЎ ETF з ”з©¶еңҳйҡҠеј·иӘҝпјҢй«ҳжҒҜеһӢ ETF еңЁеӨ§зӣӨйңҮзӣӘд№ӢйҡӣпјҢиғҪеҪ°йЎҜеҮәз©©еҒҘй«ҳжҒҜзҡ„зү№жҖ§гҖӮеёӮе ҙйңҮзӣӘжңүжҷӮпјҢйңҮзӣӘзҡ„еёӮжіҒдёӯз©©жӯҘйҒ”жҲҗиІЎеӢҷзӣ®жЁҷпјҢй«ҳжҒҜиӮЎдҫҝеҸҜзҙҚе…ҘиҖғйҮҸпјҢеҫһеӯҳиӮЎи§’еәҰдҫҶзңӢпјҢеҸ°иӮЎй«ҳжҒҜ ETF жңүжҒҜж”¶дҝқиӯ·пјҢд№ҹйҒ©еҗҲдҪңзӮәз©©еҒҘжҠ•иіҮд»ҘеҸҠйҖўдҪҺеҲҶжү№еёғеұҖзҡ„еҘҪжҠ•иіҮе·Ҙе…·гҖӮ

зҫӨзӣҠеҸ°зҒЈзІҫйҒёй«ҳжҒҜ ETF з¶“зҗҶдәәи¬қжҳҺеҝ—иЎЁзӨәпјҢеӣһжӯёеҹәжң¬йқўпјҢеҸ°зҒЈз¬¬дёҖеӯЈз¶“жҝҹжҲҗй•·зҺҮ 14.55%пјҢдё»иЁҲзёҪиҷ•дёҠдҝ® 2026 е№ҙз¶“жҝҹжҲҗй•·зҺҮ 9.64%пјҢйЎҜзӨәеҸ°иӮЎеҜҰиіӘз¶“жҝҹжҲҗй•·и¶ід»Ҙж”Ҝж’җд»Ҡе№ҙеҸ°иӮЎжјІе№…гҖӮзӣ®еүҚж”Ҝж’җеҸ°иӮЎжңҖйҮҚиҰҒзҡ„ AI з”ўжҘӯи¶ЁеӢўпјҢдёҚи«–еҫһжңҖдёҠжёёзҡ„з®—еҠӣеҹәе»әд»ҘиҮізөӮз«Ҝзҡ„и»ҹй«”жңҚеӢҷеҸҠеҖӢдәәзЎ¬й«”йңҖжұӮзҡҶиҷ•й«ҳеәҰжҲҗй•·зӢҖж…ӢпјҢеҸ°иӮЎзӣ®еүҚеғ…жҳҜиҷ•еңЁзҹӯжңҹдј°еҖјеўһйҖҹй«ҳж–јдјҒжҘӯеёійқўеғ№ж јеўһйҖҹзҡ„йҒҺеәҰйҡҺж®өпјҢж•ҙй«”и¶ЁеӢўдёҰжңӘж”№и®ҠжҲ–дёӢдҝ®пјҢзҹӯз·ҡдҝ®жӯЈеҸӘжҳҜдј°еҖјдҝ®жӯЈиҲҮж“ жіЎжІ«зҡ„йҒҺзЁӢпјҢиҖҢйқһй•·з·ҡеӨҡй ӯзҡ„зөӮзөҗгҖӮеҸ°иӮЎдёӯй•·жңҹеҫҢеёӮиЎЁзҸҫд»ҚеҸҜжңҹпјҢжҠ•иіҮдәәеҸҜйҖўдҪҺеҲҶжү№еёғеұҖгҖӮ

news.Title: еҸ°иӮЎи·Ң76й»һз•ҷ1143й»һй•·дёӢеҪұз·ҡгҖҖдё»еӢ•ETFиҲҮAIиӮЎж’җзӣӨ

news.AI_Summary:
вҖў еҸ—зҫҺдјҠең°з·Јж”ҝжІ»йўЁйҡӘеҪұйҹҝпјҢеҸ°иӮЎж—©зӣӨдёҖеәҰйҮҚжҢ«йҖҫ1200й»һпјҢйҡӘе®Ҳ42000й»һй—ңеҚЎгҖӮ
вҖў дҪҺжҺҘиІ·зӣӨж№§е…Ҙдё»еӢ•ејҸETFиҲҮAIж—ҸзҫӨпјҢи·ҢеӢўж”¶ж–ӮпјҢзөӮе ҙеҠ ж¬ҠжҢҮж•ёж”¶43149.46й»һпјҢдёӢи·Ң76.08й»һпјҢз•ҷдёӢ1143й»һй•·дёӢеҪұз·ҡгҖӮ
вҖў жҲҗдәӨеҖјйҒ”1е…Ҷ2579.75е„„е…ғпјҢйӣ»еӯҗйЎһиӮЎжҢҮж•ёдёӢи·Ң0.2%пјҢеҚҠе°Һй«”жҢҮж•ёзҝ»зҙ…пјҢйҮ‘иһҚеҸҠж«ғиІ·жҢҮж•ёдёҠжјІгҖӮ
вҖў еҸ°з©Қйӣ»йҷӨжҒҜ6е…ғеҫҢиҝ…йҖҹеЎ«жҒҜпјҢзөӮе ҙж”¶е№ізӣӨ2250е…ғпјҢжү®жј”ж’җзӣӨи§’иүІпјӣиҒҜзҷјз§‘гҖҒеҸ°йҒ”йӣ»дёӢи·ҢгҖӮ
вҖў AIжҰӮеҝөиӮЎж—©зӣӨи·Ңе№…дёҖеәҰйҒ”5%пјҢеҚҲзӣӨеҫҢи·Ңе№…ж”¶ж–Ӯпјӣй«ҳиӮЎжҒҜгҖҒз¶ иғҪгҖҒз”ҹжҠҖж—ҸзҫӨзӣёе°ҚжҠ—и·ҢгҖӮ
news.Keywords: ең°з·Јж”ҝжІ»йўЁйҡӘ,й•·дёӢеҪұз·ҡ,дё»еӢ•ејҸETF,AIж—ҸзҫӨ,ж¬ҠеҖјиӮЎж’җзӣӨ

вҖў еҸ—зҫҺиӮЎз§‘жҠҖиӮЎдҝ®жӯЈгҖҒйҖҡиҶЁз–‘ж…®иҲҮдёӯжқұең°з·Јж”ҝжІ»еҚҮжә«еҪұйҹҝпјҢеҸ°иӮЎзӣӨдёӯеҠҮзғҲйңҮзӣӘпјҢй«ҳдҪҺжҢҜе№…йҒ”1457й»һгҖӮ
вҖў зөӮе ҙеҠ ж¬ҠжҢҮж•ёдёӢи·Ң76.08й»һпјҢж”¶43149.46й»һпјҢжҲҗдәӨйҮ‘йЎҚ1е…Ҷ2579е„„е…ғпјҢйЎҜзӨәй«ҳжӘ”зұҢзўјжҸӣжүӢеҠҮзғҲгҖӮ
вҖў ж«ғиІ·жҢҮж•ёдёҠжјІ0.29%пјҢдёӯе°ҸеһӢйЎҢжқҗиӮЎиіҮйҮ‘ијӘеӢ•пјҢж«ғиІ·еҚҠе°Һй«”гҖҒйӣ»еӯҗйҖҡи·ҜиЎЁзҸҫеј·еӢўгҖӮ
вҖў еҸ°з©Қйӣ»е®ҢжҲҗеЎ«жҒҜж”¶е№ізӣӨ2250е…ғпјҢиҒҜйӣ»еҸ—жғ жҲҗзҶҹиЈҪзЁӢйЎҢжқҗдёҠжјІ6.5%пјҢзӮәд»Ҡж—Ҙж’җзӣӨдә®й»һгҖӮ
вҖў иў«еӢ•е…ғд»¶иҲҮPCBж—ҸзҫӨжңүиіҮйҮ‘жүҝжҺҘпјҢдҪҶеӨҡжӘ”еҖӢиӮЎжҢҜе№…и¶…йҒҺдёҖжҲҗпјҢйЎҜзӨәзұҢзўјжҸӣжүӢжҝҖзғҲпјӣзҹҪе…үеӯҗж—ҸзҫӨиЎЁзҸҫз–ІејұгҖӮ

news.Title: Is Broadcom Stock Now a Better Buy Than Nvidia?
news.Content: Broadcom (NASDAQ: AVGO) has had a solid 2026, but a rough couple of weeks recently. It's up more than 13% for the year, but it used to be up around 40% prior to its earnings announcement. Now, it's down around 20% from its all-time highs. Prior to the sell-off, it was pretty clear that Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) was a better buy than Broadcom, but now that there has ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Nvidia (NVDA) and Broadcom (AVGO) are key players in the AI chip market, with Nvidia dominating GPU usage in data centers.
вҖў Broadcom focuses on custom AI chips tailored for specific workloads, offering a potentially lower price point for optimized tasks.
вҖў Nvidia's overall revenue grew 85% YoY in Q1 FY2027, while Broadcom's AI semiconductor revenue grew 143% YoY, with projections of $100 billion+ by FY2027.
вҖў Despite Broadcom's strong AI segment growth, Nvidia is considered the faster-growing and cheaper stock based on forward P/E ratio.
вҖў The article concludes that Nvidia remains the better investment due to its established market position and faster overall growth, despite Broadcom's recent price correction.
news.Keywords: AI Chips, GPUs, Custom Silicon, Valuation, Market Share

news.Title: Elon Musk Wants to Create a "Space-Faring Civilization." Here's What That Means for the SpaceX IPO.
news.Content: With the SpaceX initial public offering (IPO) just days away, the company's IPO prospectus has become required reading for investors around the world. In this 380-page document, several incredibly bold claims are made. One of the boldest is at the start of the document, where SpaceX CEO Elon Musk outlines his vision for the company: Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў SpaceX's IPO prospectus outlines Elon Musk's ambitious vision for a 'space-faring civilization' and making life multiplanetary.
вҖў Musk's historical predictions, particularly for Tesla's autonomous driving capabilities, have often been overly optimistic and not fully realized.
вҖў Despite the ambitious rhetoric, SpaceX's long-term value is not solely dependent on achieving a multiplanetary civilization.
вҖў The company's projected total addressable market includes significant value from Starlink ($1.6 trillion) and AI ($26.5 trillion), with only $370 billion related to rockets.
вҖў SpaceX's value proposition is supported by its core businesses like Starlink and its potential in AI, irrespective of the multiplanetary vision.
news.Keywords: SpaceX, IPO, Elon Musk, Vision, Total Addressable Market

news.Title: Is Redwire a Millionaire-Maker Stock?
news.Content: Elon Musk's space industrial giant, SpaceX , is expected to launch its much-anticipated initial public offering (IPO) this week. But while the stock isn't available yet, that hasn't stopped eager investors from bidding up the valuations of other space-related companies. Redwire (NYSE: RDW) is a great example, with its share up by a whopping 105% so far this year. Let's dig deeper to find out if this ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Redwire (RDW) has seen a 105% stock price increase year-to-date, driven by tailwinds in defense tech and space infrastructure.
вҖў The company's defense segment, boosted by the acquisition of Edge Autonomy, focuses on autonomous drones and navigation hardware.
вҖў Redwire's space infrastructure business aims to capitalize on government outsourcing, with its technology featured in NASA's Artemis II mission.
вҖў First-quarter revenue rose 58% year-over-year to $97 million, primarily driven by the defense tech segment.
вҖў The company is facing challenges in achieving profitability, with operating losses widening due to high expenses, and recently announced a $500 million stock issuance, leading to potential dilution.
news.Keywords: Aerospace, Defense Tech, Space Infrastructure, Revenue Growth, Equity Dilution

news.AI_Summary:

вҖў зҫҺеңӢеңӢйҡӣиІҝжҳ“е§”е“ЎжңғпјҲITCпјүе·Іе•ҹеӢ•337иӘҝжҹҘпјҢиӢҘдҫөж¬ҠжҲҗз«ӢпјҢжңҖеҡҙйҮҚеҸҜиғҪзҰҒжӯўзӣёй—ңжҷ¶зүҮйҖІеҸЈзҫҺеңӢпјҢеҪұйҹҝзҫҺеңӢAIгҖҒйӣІз«ҜеҸҠеңӢйҳІдҫӣжҮүйҸҲгҖӮ

news.Title: The SpaceX IPO Is Days Away: Could the Stock Join the S&P 500, and How Soon?
news.Content: On Friday, SpaceX (NASDAQ: SPCX) is expected to begin trading on the Nasdaq in what would be the largest initial public offering (IPO) on record. The rocket and satellite company plans to sell about 556 million shares at $135 apiece, giving it an initial market value of about $1.77 trillion and raising about $75 billion. Investor demand has reportedly topped $250 billion -- more than three times what the company is seeking. A market value of $1.77 trillion would make SpaceX about the seventh-most valuable ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў SpaceX is preparing for a massive IPO with an initial market value of approximately $1.77 trillion, but faces hurdles for S&P 500 inclusion.
вҖў Key requirements for S&P 500 inclusion include at least 10% public float and 12 months of trading history, which SpaceX's IPO will not meet.
вҖў SpaceX also fails the profitability test, reporting significant net losses in 2025 and Q1 2026, despite its Starlink segment being profitable.
вҖў S&P Dow Jones Indices has maintained its rules regarding profitability and trading history for index inclusion, rejecting proposed waivers for megacap companies.
вҖў Tesla's lengthy S&P 500 inclusion process (2010-2020) highlights the strict adherence to earnings and trading history requirements.
news.Keywords: SpaceX, IPO, S&P 500, Profitability, Market Value

news.Title: 3 Beaten-Down AI Chip Stocks Worth a Closer Look After the Sell-Off
news.Content: It's been a brutal stretch for semiconductor investors. Last week, a wave of selling swept through the artificial intelligence (AI) chip sector, erasing about $1.3 trillion of market value from chip stocks in Friday's session alone. Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) fell about 6% that day, and Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ: AMD) dropped almost 11%. Broadcom (NASDAQ: AVGO) , whose earnings report earlier in the week helped set off the slide, has lost about a fifth of its value in a week. And now these ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў The AI chip sector experienced a significant sell-off, erasing approximately $1.3 trillion in market value, with Nvidia (NVDA), AMD (AMD), and Broadcom (AVGO) seeing notable declines.
вҖў Nvidia's Q1 FY2027 revenue increased 85% YoY to $81.6 billion, driven by 92% growth in data center revenue, and it guided for 95% YoY growth in Q2.
вҖў AMD's Q1 revenue grew 38% YoY to $10.3 billion, with its data center segment up 57%, and it guided for 46% YoY growth in Q2.
вҖў Broadcom's Q2 FY2026 revenue grew 48% YoY to $22.2 billion, with AI chip revenue up 143% to $10.8 billion; it projects $56 billion in AI chip revenue for FY2026.
вҖў Despite recent declines, the underlying businesses of these AI chip companies are showing accelerating growth, presenting potential opportunities for long-term investors.
news.Keywords: AI Chips, Semiconductor, Sell-off, Revenue Growth, Valuation

news.Title: Is It Really Safe to Invest in the S&P 500 at Record Highs? History Offers a Clear Answer.
news.Content: After falling around 9% from its all-time high in March, the S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) sharply reversed course and continued setting new records in April and May. Year to date, the index is up 9%. After double-digit gains in 2023, 2024, and 2025, investors who've missed out on any part of this rally might feel like it's too dangerous to put money in ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў The S&P 500 has reached new record highs in April and May 2026, up 9% year-to-date, following double-digit gains in prior years.
вҖў Historical data suggests that investing at market peaks is generally not detrimental for long-term investors, with eventual recovery to new highs being probable.
вҖў Even during severe bear markets (e.g., tech bubble, financial crisis, 2022), long-term investors who remained invested eventually recovered and saw gains.
вҖў The recovery time to new highs after market peaks varies, with the 2022 bear market recovering in approximately two years, while earlier peaks took longer.
вҖў The article recommends continued investment in S&P 500 ETFs for long-term investors, regardless of current market levels, provided they have a long investment horizon.
news.Keywords: S&P 500, Market Peaks, Long-term Investing, Bear Markets, ETFs

news.Title: Why Devon Energy Stock Rallied Today
news.Content: Shares of Devon Energy (NYSE: DVN) rose on Wednesday after the hydrocarbon exploration specialist provided investors with an updated operational forecast for 2026. Missed Nvidia in 2009? ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Devon Energy (DVN) shares rallied following an updated operational forecast for 2026 after its merger with Coterra Energy.
вҖў The combined company expects to produce an average of 1.38 million barrels of oil equivalent per day in 2026, with planned capital expenditures of $4.9 billion.
вҖў Devon Energy prioritizes cost-efficiency and free cash flow generation, targeting $1 billion in annual pre-tax cost savings by the end of 2027.
вҖў The company plans to allocate capital towards debt reduction ($1.25 billion) and shareholder returns, including a quarterly dividend and $8 billion in stock buybacks.
вҖў Management highlighted the strength of the combined platform as one of the largest and most efficient E&P companies.
news.Keywords: Oil and Gas, Merger, Operational Forecast, Shareholder Returns, Free Cash Flow

news.Title: Oracle Just Revealed a Massive $638 Billion Backlog. Here's Why the Stock Fell Anyway.
news.Content: Oracle (NYSE: ORCL) reported its results for the fiscal fourth quarter of 2026 (the period ended May 31) after the market closed on Wednesday, and by most measures, the report was excellent. Revenue rose 21% year over year to $19.2 billion, and the company's backlog of contracted future revenue swelled to a record $638 billion. Management also stood by its forecast for revenue to reach $90 billion in fiscal 2027 -- growth of about 34%. Yet shares of the cloud and database giant slipped about ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Oracle (ORCL) reported strong Q4 FY2026 results with revenue up 21% YoY to $19.2 billion and a record backlog of $638 billion.
вҖў Despite excellent results, the stock fell due to concerns about the significant capital expenditures required for its AI data center build-out, estimated at $40 billion for FY2027.
вҖў Oracle's cloud infrastructure revenue grew 93% YoY to $5.8 billion, with total cloud revenue up 47% YoY to $9.9 billion.
вҖў Capital expenditures in FY2026 totaled $55.7 billion, resulting in negative free cash flow of $23.7 billion, necessitating substantial debt and equity raises.
вҖў While customers are contributing $75 billion towards AI contracts (prepaid and supplied hardware), the company's aggressive expansion strategy raises concerns about its financial sustainability.
news.Keywords: Cloud Computing, AI Data Centers, Backlog, Capital Expenditures, Financial Sustainability

news.Title: Is This the End of Cardano?
news.Content: When the founder of a blockchain publicly suggests the second half of the year will be "very hard" for his own creation, holders tend to notice, and then they often dump their coins. news.Title: Apollo & Blackstone Inc. (BX) Seal $35B Anthropic AI Deal
news.Content: Blackstone Inc. (NYSE: BX ) is a deep value stock to invest in now . On June 6, 2026, Apollo Global Management (NYSE:APO) and Blackstone (NYSE:BX) finalized a $35 billion financing package ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Apollo Global Management and Blackstone finalized a $35 billion financing package for Anthropic.
вҖў The deal is one of the largest private credit transactions on record and will fund Google's custom TPUs for Anthropic's AI computing capacity.
вҖў The financing package includes senior notes backed by Broadcom (AVGO), which helped secure investment-grade ratings.
вҖў A special-purpose vehicle will purchase chips using debt and equity, then lease them to Anthropic, with lease payments servicing the debt.
вҖў Broadcom CEO Hock Tan confirmed collaboration on the AI XPV platform, aiming to deploy 20 GW of compute capacity by 2028.
news.Keywords: private credit, AI infrastructure, data centers, semiconductors, compute capacity

news.Title: Nvidia Corp (NVDA) Strengthens AI Platform with Micron HBM4
news.Content: Nvidia Corp (NASDAQ: NVDA ) is one of the best data center stocks to invest in according to billionaires . Nvidia shares are up 14% over the past month and up more than 48% over the past year. On June 6, Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) secured certification for its HBM4 memory on Nvidia Corp (NASDAQ:NVDA)вҖҷs upcoming ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Micron Technology (MU) has secured certification for its HBM4 memory on Nvidia Corp (NVDA)'s upcoming Vera Rubin AI platform.
вҖў This positions Micron as a key supplier for Nvidia's next-generation AI systems, alongside Samsung and SK Hynix.
вҖў The certification highlights the critical role of high-bandwidth memory (HBM) in powering advanced AI accelerators and data center workloads.
вҖў Nvidia's strategy to secure diversified, high-performance components for its AI factory architecture is reinforced by this partnership.
вҖў The news underscores the accelerating demand for advanced memory driven by increased AI spending.
news.Keywords: AI infrastructure, high-bandwidth memory, next-generation AI systems, supply chain diversification, data center workloads

news.Title: Here's What History Says to Expect for Ethereum This Summer
news.Content: If you ask a longtime Ethereum (CRYPTO: ETH) holder how he or she feels about the coin's performance during the summer, the person might talk about that one time in 2021 when the coin was on fire -- and then wince when starting to think about how it performs pretty much every other year. ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Ethereum (CRYPTO: ETH) historically experiences a decline during the summer months, with median returns being negative in June, July, August, and September.
вҖў The current trading price is near $1,675.66, below its August 2025 peak, amidst extreme pessimism and fear in the crypto market.
вҖў While historical seasonal patterns suggest further declines, explosive upward movements (over 49%) have occurred in specific summer months in prior years (2020, 2022, 2025).
вҖў The article suggests dollar-cost averaging (DCA) as a strategy for long-term conviction holders during this traditionally weak season.
вҖў Increased risks from hacks targeting DeFi and a weak crypto market in 2026 make the coin riskier, with near-term upside questionable, suggesting a cautious approach.
news.Keywords: cryptocurrency, seasonal trends, market sentiment, risk assessment, investment strategy

news.Title: 2 Bargain Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks to Buy Now Down Around 25% From Their All-Time Highs
news.Content: Finding artificial intelligence (AI) stocks that are priced at bargain levels isn't easy. Most companies in the industry are either fairly valued or trade at a premium. Relatively few of them look like bargains, but I think there are a handful that could justifiably be labeled that way. Both Meta Platforms (NASDAQ: META) and Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) are down by ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Meta Platforms (META) and Microsoft (MSFT) are identified as potential bargain AI stocks, both trading around 25% below their all-time highs.
вҖў Meta's Q1 revenue grew 33% year-over-year, driven by its advertising business, but significant AI capital expenditure ($135 billion midpoint guidance) raises investor concerns about ROI.
вҖў Microsoft's AI business grew 123% year-over-year, with an annual run rate of $37 billion, and its Azure cloud unit grew 40% year-over-year.
вҖў Meta trades at 18 times forward earnings, cheaper than the S&P 500 (22.2x), while Microsoft trades at 21.3 times fiscal 2027 earnings, also below the S&P 500.
вҖў Microsoft is seen as having a clearer path to AI monetization compared to Meta, which is heavily reliant on advertising revenue.
news.Keywords: AI stocks, bargain valuation, revenue growth, capital expenditure, forward earnings

news.Title: Why Marvell Technology Stock Sank Today
news.Content: Marvell Technology (NASDAQ: MRVL) stock moved lower in Wednesday's trading as investors reduced exposure to growth-dependent tech plays. The company's share price declined 5.4 % in a daily trading session that played host to a 1.6% contraction for ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Marvell Technology (MRVL) stock declined by 5.4% due to investors reducing exposure to growth-dependent tech plays.
вҖў The broader tech sector experienced sell-offs, with the S&P 500 contracting 1.6% and the Nasdaq Composite sliding 2%.
вҖў Investors reacted to the latest Consumer Price Index (CPI) data showing annual inflation at 4.2% and core CPI at 2.9%, with concerns about persistent inflation.
вҖў Renewed conflict dynamics in the Iran war are contributing to fears of sustained higher energy prices and inflation.
вҖў The macroeconomic outlook, including potential Federal Reserve interest rate hikes, creates a less favorable valuation environment for growth stocks like Marvell.
news.Keywords: growth stocks, tech sell-off, inflation data, macroeconomic outlook, interest rate risk

news.Title: Meta Expands AI Partnership With Reliance
news.Content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus .

Meta Platforms ( META , Financials ) is strengthening its partnership with Reliance Industries as both companies look to benefit from India's growing demand for AI infrastructure.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 2 Warning Sign with META.

Is META fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

Under the agreement, Reliance will build a 168-megawatt data center in Jamnagar, India, and Meta will lease capacity from the site. The companies did not disclose financial terms or a launch date.

The deal makes sense for both sides. Meta needs more computing power to support its AI tools and services. Reliance already has land, power and infrastructure in Jamnagar, giving it a strong position to support large data center projects.

The partnership also builds on an existing relationship. Meta is already an investor in Reliance's Jio Platforms, and the two companies have worked together on AI tools for businesses using Meta's Llama models.

For investors, the main point is clear: Meta is still spending heavily to build the infrastructure behind its AI ambitions, and India is becoming a bigger part of that strategy.

news.Title: TSMC Posts 30% Revenue Growth as AI Trade Cools
news.Content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus .

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company ( NYSE:TSM ) reported May 2026 consolidated net revenue of NT$416.975 billion ($13.17 billion), up 30.1% from May 2025 and 1.5% from April 2026. Cumulative revenue for January through May 2026 reached NT$1,961.804 billion ($62 billion), a 30.0% increase compared to the same period last year.

The results extend TSMC's streak of robust year-over-year growth, driven by sustained demand for advanced chips used in AI infrastructure, smartphones, and high-performance computing.

TSMC's numbers land as the broader AI trade takes a breather. The Nasdaq fell 4% last Friday, its worst session since early 2025, as investors began reassessing chip valuations. The monthly figures failed to lift sentiment, with shares down 2.60% in premarket despite the strong numbers.

Select Growth mainly targets leading U.S. businesses, driving positive structural changes. U.S. large-cap growth stocks fell in the first quarter. Sharp dispersion driven by AI advances marked the quarter, but late in the quarter, geopolitical tensions with Iran caused a broad-based risk-off move across the market. AI continued to influence market behavior, with AI-related investments increasing dispersion and shifting capital to asset-heavy sectors benefiting from AI infrastructure demand, which faced less disruption risk. While equities struggled, corporate fundamentals remained strong. Select Growth underperformed the Russell 1000 Growth Index, returning -12.9% vs. -9.8%, due to concerns about AI disruption affecting sector and stock choices. The Strategy's focus on higher-growth, asset-light, service businesses faced challenges as markets rotated toward more capital-intensive, lower-risk sectors. Underweights in cyclical and defensive sectors slightly hurt relative results amid the broader market shift. In addition, please check the StrategyвҖҷs top five holdings to know its best picks in 2026.

In its first-quarter 2026 investor letter, Sands Capital Select Growth Strategy highlighted Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (NYSE: TSM ) as a notable performance contributor. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (NYSE:TSM) is the worldвҖҷs leading contract chip manufacturer, producing advanced semiconductors for major global technology companies. On June 9, 2026, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (NYSE:TSM) closed at $427.92 per share. One-month return of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (NYSE:TSM) was 7.03%, and its shares gained 99.87% over the past 52 weeks. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (NYSE:TSM) has a market capitalization of $2.22 trillion.

Sands Capital Select Growth Strategy stated the following regarding Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (NYSE:TSM) in its Q1 2026 investor letter:

"Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited's (NYSE:TSM) latest results highlighted accelerating AI demand and reinforced the companyвҖҷs position as a primary beneficiary of that trend. The update included two new data points that strengthened our outlook for AI accelerator demand. First, TSMC Chairman C.C. Wei said he personally spoke with the companyвҖҷs largest hyperscaler AI customers and concluded that their requests for additional capacity were supported by end demand. Given TSMCвҖҷs historically conservative posture, we view this as a meaningful signal. Second, the company reinforced that confidence with a materially higher capital expenditure plan of $54 billion for 2026, well above consensus estimates of $47.8 billion, to support expected AI-driven demand. TSMC also reported results and issued guidance that exceeded our expectations and those of consensus. Management now expects AI-related revenue to grow at a 55 percent annualized rate through 2029, up from 45 percent previously, driven by both higher volumes and improved pricing. While peers continue to invest, we believe TSMCвҖҷs yields, scale, and surrounding ecosystem continue to differentiate it for high-performance GPUs and leading CPUs. Planned expansions in Arizona, including additional advanced fabs and a U.S.-based advanced packaging facility, should also ease prior capacity concerns."

news.Title: STMicro gains as BofA upgrades stock to Buy with Street-high price target
news.Content: Investing.com -- Bank of America upgraded STMicroelectronics (EPA:STMPA) (NYSE:STM) shares to Buy from Neutral on Tuesday, setting a Street-high price target of вӮ¬86/$100, arguing the market is underestimating the chipmakerвҖҷs earnings power over the next two to three years. The stock rose ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Bank of America upgraded STMicroelectronics (STM) to 'Buy' with a Street-high price target of вӮ¬86/$100, citing underestimation of earnings power over the next 2-3 years.
вҖў Key drivers for the upgrade include STM's growing position in optical interconnects for data centers (projected revenue growth from $670M to $2.3B by 2028), exposure to the LEO satellite market, and a nascent recovery in automotive/industrial demand.
вҖў BofA forecasts significant gross margin expansion from 37.3% to 46% by 2028, driven by falling unused capacity charges, improved pricing, and scaling of higher-margin businesses.
вҖў The upgrade is supported by projected EPS of $1.57, $3.53, and $4.63 for 2026, 2027, and 2028, respectively, which are 30-43% above consensus.
news.Keywords: semiconductor, earnings power, optical interconnects, LEO satellites, gross margin expansion

news.Title: Texas Instruments (TXN) вҖ“ Among the 10 Best Dividend Stocks to Buy According to D. E. Shaw
news.Content: Texas Instruments Incorporated (NASDAQ: TXN ) is included among the 10 Best Dividend Stocks to Buy According to D. E. Shaw . On June 9, Wells Fargo raised its price recommendation on Texas Instruments Incorporated (NASDAQ:TXN) ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Texas Instruments (TXN) has been named among the '10 Best Dividend Stocks to Buy' by D. E. Shaw.
вҖў Wells Fargo raised its price recommendation to $300 from $260, reiterating an Equal Weight rating, and sees physical AI as a long-term opportunity for analog/mixed-signal semiconductors.
вҖў BofA increased its price target to $370 from $320 with a Buy rating, noting that TXN's multi-year content gains and recovery in industrial/automotive markets are underappreciated.
вҖў The company's core industrial and automotive markets are showing signs of a cyclical tailwind, further supported by AI-related data demand.
news.Keywords: dividend stocks, analog semiconductors, AI opportunity, industrial market, automotive market

news.Title: Broader Market Settles Lower as Chip Stocks Sink
news.Content: The S&P 500 Index ($SPX) (SPY) on Tuesday closed down -0.26%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average ($DOWI) (DIA) closed up +0.17%, and the Nasdaq 100 Index ($IUXX) (QQQ) closed down -1.12%. June E-mini S&P futures (ESM26) fell -0.36%, and June E-mini Nasdaq futures (NQM26) fell -1.15%. Stock indexes gave up an early advance on Tuesday and retreated, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 falling to 5-week lows. news.AI_Summary:
вҖў The broader market, including the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100, closed lower on Tuesday, with chip stocks experiencing a sharp sell-off.
вҖў Investors rotated out of the semiconductor sector, contributing to the market's decline.
вҖў Weakness in software stocks and a significant drop in WTI crude oil prices also weighed on the overall market.
вҖў Positive economic news included easing trade deficit and stronger-than-expected existing home sales, while geopolitical tensions and news regarding Iran and Israel created market uncertainty.

news.Title: Can KLA Outgrow Chip Equipment Market Through 2030?
news.Content: KLA Corporation KLAC is strengthening its position in the chip equipment market as AI-driven semiconductor complexity makes process control increasingly critical to advanced manufacturing. The companyвҖҷs long-term growth case is tied less to broad capacity additions alone and more to the rising need for inspection, metrology and yield-optimization tools as chips become more complex, valuable and difficult to manufacture. KLAвҖҷs updated long-term framework ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў KLA Corporation (KLAC) is strengthening its position in the chip equipment market, driven by AI-driven semiconductor complexity.
вҖў The company raised its revenue CAGR target to 13-17% through 2030, expecting to outgrow the wafer equipment market.
вҖў Growth is supported by increasing demand for inspection, metrology, and yield-optimization tools, particularly in advanced packaging.
вҖў Revenues tied to advanced packaging are projected to rise from $635 million in 2025 to $1 billion in 2026.

news.Title: Microchip or Fabrinet: Which Tech Stock Is a Better Buy Now?
news.Content: Two companies currently drawing significant investor interest in the Zacks Computer and Technology sector are Microchip Technology MCHP and Fabrinet FN. Microchip Technology develops, manufactures and sells smart, connected and secure embedded control solutions. The company focuses on general-purpose and specialized 8-bit, 16-bit and 32-bit mixed-signal microcontrollers, microprocessors, analog, FPGA and memory products. вҖў MCHP is benefiting from AI investments, with strong sales growth in its Gen 4 and Gen 5 data center products and the launch of a 3nm-based PCIe Gen 6 switch.
вҖў Fabrinet is experiencing growth from automation and AI, serving as a key manufacturing partner for advanced optical transceivers and HPC clusters.
вҖў Fabrinet expects Q4 fiscal 2026 revenues between $1.25 billion and $1.29 billion, supported by its debt-free balance sheet.
news.Keywords: embedded control solutions, optical packaging, AI infrastructure, data center products, manufacturing services

news.Title: Qualcomm vs. Silicon Motion: Which Semiconductor Stock Wins Now?
news.Content: Qualcomm Incorporated QCOM and Silicon Motion Technology Corporation SIMO are leading semiconductor firms with exposure to key growth markets such as smartphones, automotive electronics, AI-enabled devices and data storage. Qualcomm offers high-performance, low-power chip designs for mobile devices, PCs, XR (Extended Reality), automotive, wearables, robotics, connectivity and AI use cases. Its brands include Snapdragon systems-on-chip, FastConnect Wi-Fi and Bluetooth systems and Qualcomm-branded 4G, 5G and IoT equipment. The company is currently pursuing the integration of on-device generative AI into all of its product lines. Silicon Motion is a leading developer of microcontroller ICs for NAND flash storage devices. The semiconductor ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Qualcomm (QCOM) and Silicon Motion (SIMO) are key semiconductor firms with exposure to smartphones, automotive, AI, and data storage.
вҖў Qualcomm is integrating on-device generative AI into its product lines and sees traction in edge networking and automotive V2X communication.
вҖў Silicon Motion is a leading supplier of microcontroller ICs for NAND flash storage devices and expects its 3D SSD controllers to be a growth driver.
вҖў Qualcomm faces competition from Intel in the AI PC market and expects near-term softness due to OEM inventory management and US-China trade tensions.

news.Title: 5 Stocks to Boost Your Portfolio on Soaring Semiconductor Sales
news.Content:
Investors have lately been rotating out of tech stocks and taking refuge in defensive assets, as a surge in oil prices owing to the continued tensions in the Middle East has raised fears of inflation rising further. However, the enthusiasm surrounding artificial intelligence has continued to help AI-focused companies.

High demand for AI chips has been boosting semiconductor stocks, which have primarily been responsible for the broader market rally over the past three years. Given this scenario, it would be ideal to invest in semiconductor stocks, such as Analog Devices ADI, NXP Semiconductors NXPI, Microchip Technology MCHP, Texas Instruments TXN and ASML Holding N.V. ASML, which have great potential for growth this year.

Semiconductor Revenues Surging Steadily

Global semiconductor sales totaled $110.5 billion in April, jumping 11% sequentially from March’s total of $99.5 billion, the Semiconductor Industry Association (SIA) reported. Year over year, semiconductor sales soared 93.9% $56.9 billion in April 2025.

“Global semiconductor sales increased on a month-to-month basis for the 14 th consecutive month in April, and the global market continues to notch robust year-to-year growth driven by sales into the Asia-Pacific region, the Americas, and China,” John Neuffer, SIA president and CEO, said.

The robust jump in April follows a stellar first quarter when semiconductor sales totaled $298.5 billion, increasing 25% sequentially.

Semiconductors have become a key part of nearly every modern and developing technology, from IoT and 6G to artificial intelligence. Growing demand from the automotive sector has also helped boost chip sales. Meanwhile, the fast-growing AI market — which is still in its early stages — is prompting major technology companies to invest billions of dollars in development and infrastructure.

With big tech firms continuing to spend aggressively on AI infrastructure, investor confidence in semiconductor companies has remained strong.

The SIA also backed the World Semiconductor Trade Statistics’ (“WSTS”) Spring 2026 global semiconductor sales forecast. The WSTS projects the global semiconductor market to grow 90% this year, reaching $1.51 trillion.

5 Semiconductor Stocks With Upside

Analog Devices

Analog Devices is an original equipment manufacturer of semiconductor devices, specifically analog, mixed-signal and digital signal processing (“DSP”) integrated circuits. ADI’s product line comprises amplifiers and comparators; analog to digital converters; digital to analog converters; video encoders and decoders; embedded processing products and DSPs; MEMS and temperature sensors; RF/IF components and converters; power and thermal management ICs, audio/video converters, amplifiers, CODECs, filters and processors. Analog Devices also offers analog, digital and RF switches and multiplexers; analog microcontrollers; clock and timing products.

Story Continues

Analog Devices’ expected earnings growth rate for the current year is 59.3%. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for current-year earnings has improved 0.1% over the past 60 days. ADI currently carries a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy). You can see the complete list of today’s Zacks #1 Rank (Strong Buy) stocks here .

NXP Semiconductors

NXP Semiconductors provides high-performance, mixed-signal and standard product solutions that leverage its RF, analog, power management, interface, security and digital processing expertise. NXPI seems well-positioned to capitalize on the level 2-5 automotive market. Additionally, NXP Semiconductors is the leader in general-purpose microcontrollers and application processors in industrial and IoT markets.

NXP Semiconductors’ expected earnings growth rate for the current year is 25.1%. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for current-year earnings has improved 5.7% over the past 60 days. NXPI carries a Zacks Rank #2 at present.

Microchip Technology

Microchip Technology has been consistently benefiting from its strength in the analog and microcontroller businesses. MCHP’s dominance in 8, 16, and 32-bit PIC microcontrollers remains a major driver of top-line and bookings growth. Microchip Technology has acquired notable companies like Tektronix, Microsemi and Atmel to add strength to its product offerings.

Microchip Technology’s expected earnings growth rate for the current year is 84.2%. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for current-year earnings has improved 18% over the past 60 days. MCHP currently has a Zacks Rank #1.

Texas Instruments

Texas Instruments is an original equipment manufacturer of analog, mixed-signal and digital signal-processing integrated circuits. TXN has manufacturing and design facilities, including wafer fabrication and assembly/test operations in North America, Asia and Europe. Management strategy has been to build assets that would be fully utilized through their lifetimes and outsource any excess demand in peak situations to outside foundries.

Texas Instruments’ expected earnings growth rate for the current year is 40.6%. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for current-year earnings has improved 20.6% over the past 60 days. Currently, TXN carries a Zacks Rank #2.

news.Title: Is Rambus Inc. (RMBS) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
news.Content: Is RMBS a good stock to buy? We came across a bullish thesis on Rambus Inc. on Valueinvestorsclub.com by IdeaLogue. In this article, we will summarize the bullsвҖҷ thesis on RMBS. Rambus Inc.'s share was trading at $148.02 as of May 28 th . RMBSвҖҷs trailing P/E was 70.47 according to Yahoo Finance. Photo by ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Rambus Inc. (RMBS) is presented as a potentially undervalued beneficiary of the accelerating AI infrastructure buildout, particularly due to rising CPU demand and upcoming MRDIMM chipset.
вҖў The company's business model includes highly profitable patent licensing and silicon IP segments, generating recurring revenue, alongside a rapidly growing product segment.
вҖў Rambus has significantly increased its market share in register clock drivers (RCDs) for server memory modules from zero in 2018 to approximately 45% currently.
вҖў The MRDIMM technology represents a substantial market opportunity, with management estimates suggesting consensus forecasts are conservative; successful execution could lead to significant upside.
news.Keywords: AI infrastructure, intellectual property licensing, memory interface, market share expansion, earnings potential

news.Title: ThereвҖҷs Still Time to Invest in LumentumвҖҷs Optical Networking Momentum
news.Content: Earlier this week, shares of Lumentum (LITE) rallied from about $806 to $1,049. Nvidia (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang praised Marvel (MRVL), a maker of optical components, as the next potential trillion-dollar company. Not only did that spark a massive rally in Marvell shares, but it also sparked a rally in other optical-component stocks, like LITE. Investors are rushing to ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Lumentum (LITE) experienced a significant stock rally, driven by its role in optical networking essential for the AI boom, offering higher bandwidth and speed than copper wiring.
вҖў Nvidia has committed $2 billion to Lumentum through a multi-year agreement to accelerate advanced optical technologies and secure capacity for AI factories.
вҖў The optical networking market is projected to grow substantially, from an estimated $15 billion in 2026 to nearly $154 billion by 2028, driven by AI demand.
вҖў In its most recent quarter, Lumentum reported adjusted EPS of $2.37 (vs. $2.27 est.), revenue of $808.4 million (vs. $810M est.), and an adjusted gross margin of 47.9% (vs. 45% est.).
вҖў The company provided strong forward guidance, expecting revenue between $960 million and $1.01 billion, exceeding estimates, and EPS between $2.85 and $3.05, also above expectations.
news.Keywords: optical networking, AI infrastructure, supply-demand imbalance, revenue growth, forward guidance
